title: Bump urllib3 from 2.5.0 to 2.6.0 in /packages/python-sdk in the pip group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the /packages/python-sdk directory: [urllib3](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3). Updates `urllib3` from 2.5.0 to 2.6.0
Release notes

Sourced from urllib3's releases.

2.6.0

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Security

  • Fixed a security issue where streaming API could improperly handle highly compressed HTTP content ("decompression bombs") leading to excessive resource consumption even when a small amount of data was requested. Reading small chunks of compressed data is safer and much more efficient now. (CVE-2025-66471 reported by @​Cycloctane, 8.9 High, GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37)
  • Fixed a security issue where an attacker could compose an HTTP response with virtually unlimited links in the Content-Encoding header, potentially leading to a denial of service (DoS) attack by exhausting system resources during decoding. The number of allowed chained encodings is now limited to 5. (CVE-2025-66418 reported by @​illia-v, 8.9 High, GHSA-gm62-xv2j-4w53)

[!IMPORTANT]

  • If urllib3 is not installed with the optional urllib3[brotli] extra, but your environment contains a Brotli/brotlicffi/brotlipy package anyway, make sure to upgrade it to at least Brotli 1.2.0 or brotlicffi 1.2.0.0 to benefit from the security fixes and avoid warnings. Prefer using urllib3[brotli] to install a compatible Brotli package automatically.
  • If you use custom decompressors, please make sure to update them to respect the changed API of urllib3.response.ContentDecoder.

Features

  • Enabled retrieval, deletion, and membership testing in HTTPHeaderDict using bytes keys. (#3653)
  • Added host and port information to string representations of HTTPConnection. (#3666)
  • Added support for Python 3.14 free-threading builds explicitly. (#3696)

Removals

  • Removed the HTTPResponse.getheaders() method in favor of HTTPResponse.headers. Removed the HTTPResponse.getheader(name, default) method in favor of HTTPResponse.headers.get(name, default). (#3622)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed redirect handling in urllib3.PoolManager when an integer is passed for the retries parameter. (#3649)
  • Fixed HTTPConnectionPool when used in Emscripten with no explicit port. (#3664)
  • Fixed handling of SSLKEYLOGFILE with expandable variables. (#3700)

Misc

  • Changed the zstd extra to install backports.zstd instead of zstandard on Python 3.13 and before. (#3693)
  • Improved the performance of content decoding by optimizing BytesQueueBuffer class. (#3710)
  • Allowed building the urllib3 package with newer setuptools-scm v9.x. (#3652)
  • Ensured successful urllib3 builds by setting Hatchling requirement to ≥ 1.27.0. (#3638)
Changelog

Sourced from urllib3's changelog.

2.6.0 (2025-12-05)

Security

  • Fixed a security issue where streaming API could improperly handle highly compressed HTTP content ("decompression bombs") leading to excessive resource consumption even when a small amount of data was requested. Reading small chunks of compressed data is safer and much more efficient now. (GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37 <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/security/advisories/GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37>__)
  • Fixed a security issue where an attacker could compose an HTTP response with virtually unlimited links in the Content-Encoding header, potentially leading to a denial of service (DoS) attack by exhausting system resources during decoding. The number of allowed chained encodings is now limited to 5. (GHSA-gm62-xv2j-4w53 <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/security/advisories/GHSA-gm62-xv2j-4w53>__)

.. caution::

  • If urllib3 is not installed with the optional urllib3[brotli] extra, but your environment contains a Brotli/brotlicffi/brotlipy package anyway, make sure to upgrade it to at least Brotli 1.2.0 or brotlicffi 1.2.0.0 to benefit from the security fixes and avoid warnings. Prefer using urllib3[brotli] to install a compatible Brotli package automatically.

  • If you use custom decompressors, please make sure to update them to respect the changed API of urllib3.response.ContentDecoder.

Features

  • Enabled retrieval, deletion, and membership testing in HTTPHeaderDict using bytes keys. ([#3653](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3653) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3653>__)
  • Added host and port information to string representations of HTTPConnection. ([#3666](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3666) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3666>__)
  • Added support for Python 3.14 free-threading builds explicitly. ([#3696](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3696) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3696>__)

Removals

  • Removed the HTTPResponse.getheaders() method in favor of HTTPResponse.headers. Removed the HTTPResponse.getheader(name, default) method in favor of HTTPResponse.headers.get(name, default). ([#3622](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3622) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3622>__)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed redirect handling in urllib3.PoolManager when an integer is passed for the retries parameter. ([#3649](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3649) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3649>__)
  • Fixed HTTPConnectionPool when used in Emscripten with no explicit port. ([#3664](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3664) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3664>__)
  • Fixed handling of SSLKEYLOGFILE with expandable variables. ([#3700](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3700) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3700>__)

... (truncated)

title: feat: implement network out allow/deny list support
body:
## Add Network Outbound Traffic Control for Sandboxes

Users can now configure network restrictions when creating sandboxes using the new `network` configuration option with `allowOut` and `denyOut` parameters.

- [x] Requires https://github.com/e2b-dev/infra/pull/1447 to be merged and deployed first

### Key Features

- **Allow List**: Specify IP addresses, CIDR blocks that sandboxes can connect to
- **Deny List**: Specify IP addresses, CIDR blocks that sandboxes cannot connect to
- **Priority Rules**: `allowOut` takes precedence over `denyOut` when there are conflicts
- **Helper Const**: New `ALL_TRAFFIC` helper to easily deny/allow all network traffic (`0.0.0.0/0`)

### Usage Examples

**JavaScript/TypeScript:**
```typescript
import { Sandbox, ALL_TRAFFIC } from 'e2b'

// Block all traffic except specific IPs
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('template-id', {
  network: {
    denyOut: [ALL_TRAFFIC],
    allowOut: ['1.1.1.1', '8.8.8.0/24']
  }
})

// Block specific IPs only
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('template-id', {
  network: {
    denyOut: ['8.8.8.8']
  }
})
```

**Python:**
```python
from e2b import AsyncSandbox, ALL_TRAFFIC

# Block all traffic except specific IPs
sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.create(
    network={
        "allow_out": ["8.8.8.8"],
        "deny_out": [ALL_TRAFFIC]
    }
)
```


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds sandbox network allow/deny egress, optional public traffic protection with access token and host masking, updates template build/list APIs, and integrates these across JS/Python SDKs with tests.
> 
> - **SDKs (JS & Python)**:
>   - Add `network` config to sandboxes with `allowOut`, `denyOut`, `allowPublicTraffic`, `maskRequestHost`; export `ALL_TRAFFIC` helper.
>   - Surface `trafficAccessToken` on sandbox instances; wire through create/connect flows.
>   - Update exports and types in `packages/js-sdk/src/index.ts`, `sandbox/sandboxApi.ts`, Python `e2b/__init__.py`, and sandbox base.
> - **API/Spec & Generated Clients**:
>   - Extend `NewSandbox` with `network` and clarify `allow_internet_access`; add `SandboxNetworkConfig` schema.
>   - Add `trafficAccessToken` to `Sandbox` responses.
>   - Introduce `GET /templates/{templateID}` returning `TemplateWithBuilds`; split build models into `TemplateBuild` (summary) and `TemplateBuildInfo` (status/logs); adjust `GET /templates/{templateID}/builds/{buildID}/status` to return `TemplateBuildInfo`.
>   - Add pagination params (`limit`, `nextToken`) components; update `/v2/sandboxes` to use them.
>   - Regenerate JS TS types and Python client models/endpoints accordingly.
> - **Tests**:
>   - New egress control tests and public traffic token/mask tests in `packages/js-sdk/tests/sandbox/network.test.ts` and Python async/sync tests.
> - **Tooling**:
>   - Adjust openapi-typescript generation flag (`--default-non-nullable false`).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 2b7e7155b881a891d48534c75a9536198ed0e243. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Ensure that httpx transport is reused across calls by default
body:
This takes some shortcuts in order to keep backwards compatibility.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Reuses a singleton httpx transport/client across the SDK, introduces a generic retry decorator for connect calls, and refactors code/tests to use the shared clients and fixtures.
> 
> - **SDK (transport/client reuse)**
>   - Add global `limits` (configurable via `E2B_*` env vars) and switch `ApiClient` to accept `transport` instead of `limits`.
>   - Introduce `e2b.api.client_async/client_sync` with `get_transport()` (singleton) and `get_api_client()`; update `Sandbox`/`AsyncSandbox`, paginators, and sandbox APIs to use them.
>   - Remove per-class `_limits` from `SandboxBase`/`TemplateBase`; merge extra headers correctly; pass `E2b-Sandbox-Port` as string.
>   - Template build flows (sync/async) now reuse the API client's underlying httpx client for file uploads.
> - **Connect client** (`e2b_connect/client.py`)
>   - Add `_retry` decorator and apply to unary/server-stream methods; simplify reconnection logic; minor typing/headers cleanups.
> - **Tests**
>   - Add retry unit tests; introduce `sandbox_factory`/`async_sandbox_factory` and a session `event_loop`; refactor tests to use factories and shared transports; adjust tar archive expectations.
> - **Misc**
>   - Add `.editorconfig`, CLI `.envrc`, and changeset entries.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 1d359231b63dc21b6c8d797886f7d6a77c9ed6b7. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

 The `upload_file` function call is missing the required `api_client` parameter as the first argument. The function signature in `build_api.py` was changed to accept `api_client` as the first parameter, but the call site wasn't updated. This causes all arguments to be shifted incorrectly, passing `src` where `api_client` is expected.

title: Support overriding envd API URL
body:
This requires [infra#1448](https://github.com/e2b-dev/infra/pull/1448) first. 

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds support to override the sandbox API URL (E2B_SANDBOX_URL) across JS/Python SDKs, centralizes sandbox host/url logic with headers, and updates CI to build the SDK before CLI.
> 
> - **SDKs (JS & Python)**
>   - Add `sandboxUrl` support in `ConnectionConfig` (env var `E2B_SANDBOX_URL`), with new helpers `getSandboxUrl`/`getHost` and shared `envdPort`.
>   - Refactor sandbox initialization to use `ConnectionConfig.getSandboxUrl(...)` and `getHost(...)`.
>   - Always attach sandbox headers `E2b-Sandbox-Id` and `E2b-Sandbox-Port` to sandbox and connect requests.
>   - Python: thread `sandbox_url` through opts; update async/sync connect calls to pass headers; minor fix to default `headers=None` in `e2b_connect.client.Client` stream prep.
> - **CI**
>   - Build `packages/js-sdk` before `packages/cli`; set step `working-directory` for build/test.
> - **Dependencies**
>   - Point `e2b` dependency in lockfile to local `../js-sdk` link.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 5dc58171af6c170f8640f49d736dbe9c571f2b21. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: djeebus

reviews.body:
Bug: Envd API URL Not Passed During Sandbox Reconnect

 The `_cls_connect` method doesn't pass `envd_api_url` when creating the sandbox instance. When reconnecting to an existing sandbox, the envd_api_url will always default to the computed value instead of allowing users to override it. The method should accept an `envd_api_url` parameter and pass it to the `cls()` constructor call on line 672.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L648-L677 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/34e33a3d7f068cb26eb2080b7db1850129ebbb05/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L648-L677

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request.
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
Codex has been enabled to automatically review pull requests in this repo. Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. When you [sign up for Codex through ChatGPT](https://openai.com/codex), Codex can also answer questions or update the PR, like "@codex address that feedback".

Bug: API URL not honored by beta_create method

 The `beta_create` method does not accept or pass the `envd_api_url` parameter to `_create()`, even though `_create()` now accepts this parameter (line 689). This means users cannot override the envd API URL when using `beta_create`, creating an inconsistency with the regular `create` method which does support this parameter.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py#L547-L590 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/55c959ef58167fff35b3e033f79a29038e485a2c/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py#L547-L590

Bug: Inconsistent beta_create with envd_api_url parameter

 The `beta_create` method doesn't accept the `envd_api_url` parameter that was added to the `create` method and `_create` internal method. This creates an inconsistency where users can override the envd API URL with `create()` but not with `beta_create()`, even though both methods call the same `_create()` internal method that now supports this parameter.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L544-L577 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/4ae206b7cfcc87cdbcbcef602bfd901ea5c52de3/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L544-L577

Bug: Sandbox connection header inconsistency

 The `_cls_connect` method doesn't add `E2b-Sandbox-Id` and `E2b-Sandbox-Port` headers to `sandbox_headers` before creating the `ConnectionConfig`, unlike the `_create` method which adds these headers. This inconsistency means sandboxes connected via `connect()` will be missing these headers that are present when using `create()`, potentially causing issues if the infrastructure expects them.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L646-L675 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/ff1bee5c7c38bb7e093bff91dbbcdd4ac095e120/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L646-L675

Bug: Sandbox URL override feature fails.

 The constructor doesn't assign `opts?.sandboxUrl` to a class property, but `getSandboxUrl()` method references `this.sandboxUrl` on line 131. This causes the sandbox URL override feature to not work since the property is always undefined.

packages/js-sdk/src/connectionConfig.ts#L83-L97 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/6e6791b100bf8b8f51fc34df06af2a24fde0ecdb/packages/js-sdk/src/connectionConfig.ts#L83-L97

Bug: Sandbox URL: Programmatic Override Silently Ignored

 The `sandboxUrl` option in `ConnectionOpts` is documented and exposed but never stored or used in the `ConnectionConfig` constructor. When users pass `sandboxUrl` in options, it gets silently ignored because `getSandboxUrl` only checks the static environment variable getter, not an instance field. This breaks the ability to override the sandbox URL programmatically.

packages/js-sdk/src/connectionConfig.ts#L84-L99 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/75b134ea922948cd21aa5264bc2624d526ed3a90/packages/js-sdk/src/connectionConfig.ts#L84-L99

Bug: Sandbox URL option is ineffective.

 The `ConnectionConfig` constructor doesn't store or use the `sandboxUrl` option from `opts`, even though `ConnectionOpts` interface includes it and `getSandboxUrl` method exists. This means passing `sandboxUrl` via options has no effect, unlike the Python SDK which correctly handles this parameter. The constructor should store `opts?.sandboxUrl` and `getSandboxUrl` should check it before falling back to the environment variable.

packages/js-sdk/src/connectionConfig.ts#L85-L99 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/e8e26a8481d4eb328673182d7800e98e805429a8/packages/js-sdk/src/connectionConfig.ts#L85-L99

Bug: Inconsistent Sandbox Connection Headers

 The `_cls_connect` method doesn't set the `E2b-Sandbox-Id` and `E2b-Sandbox-Port` headers that are required for sandbox communication. These headers are set in the `_create` method but missing here, causing inconsistent behavior when connecting to existing sandboxes versus creating new ones.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py#L650-L676 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/1953ee305467a36624de482013e88730097ed39a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py#L650-L676

Bug: Reconnected Sandboxes Lose Identification

 The `_cls_connect` method doesn't set the `E2b-Sandbox-Id` and `E2b-Sandbox-Port` headers that are required for sandbox communication. These headers are properly set in the `_create` method but missing here, causing reconnected sandboxes to lack necessary identification headers when making requests to the sandbox API.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py#L650-L677 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/2b250968d875d09fbad9063331ee6246d76b6de7/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py#L650-L677

Bug: Missing Dependency Breaks CLI Functionality

 The `e2b` dependency was removed from the CLI package dependencies, but the CLI code extensively imports and uses `e2b` throughout multiple files (api.ts, sandbox commands, template commands, auth commands). This will cause the CLI package to fail at runtime when trying to import `e2b`.

packages/cli/package.json#L88-L89 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/e5a7aa7933b772660d4a17f0d5c7e483c7fa0817/packages/cli/package.json#L88-L89

Bug: Inconsistent Sandbox Header Handling

 The `_cls_connect` method doesn't set the `E2b-Sandbox-Id` and `E2b-Sandbox-Port` headers that are set in the `_create` method. This creates an inconsistency where sandboxes created via `create()` have these headers but sandboxes connected via `connect()` don't, potentially causing different behavior or failures when the backend expects these headers.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L655-L677 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8131eb4781aba81d5467c5e4de004d548593dfdd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L655-L677

Bug: Sandbox Connection Headers: Inconsistency Bug

 The `_cls_connect` method is missing the `E2b-Sandbox-Id` and `E2b-Sandbox-Port` headers that are added in the `_create` method. When connecting to an existing sandbox, these headers won't be sent, causing inconsistent behavior compared to newly created sandboxes. The headers should be added to `sandbox_headers` before creating the `ConnectionConfig`.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L655-L677 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/b4d48c3af61563e66551c9334a40d236c6f1a04d/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py#L655-L677

commits.message: use headers to route to sandboxes

title: Fix ignore files in file upload and added tests
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> Ensure file uploads honor ignore patterns (incl. .dockerignore), refactor file discovery/tar streaming in JS/Python SDKs, and add comprehensive tests.
> 
> - **File upload behavior**:
>   - JS/TS and Python uploads now pass `ignorePatterns` (merged from `fileIgnorePatterns` and `.dockerignore`) to tar creation, ensuring ignored files aren’t uploaded.

> - **Tests**:
>   - Add extensive tests for `getAllFilesInPath` and `tar_file_stream` in JS and Python, covering ignore patterns, directories, sorting, and symlinks.

title: Improve cache hit consistency accross environments
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> Stabilizes copy-file hashing by excluding uid/gid/mtime and updates tests/build helpers to use skip cache consistently.
> 
> - **Caching/Hashing**:
>   - JS (`packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts`) and Python (`packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py`): `calculateFilesHash` excludes `uid`, `gid`, and `mtime`; only `mode` and `size` plus paths/content are hashed for environment-stable cache keys.
> - **Tests**:
>   - JS tests: propagate `.skipCache()` in templates and pass `{ skipCache: true }` to `buildTemplate`; add `options?: { skipCache?: boolean }` to `buildTemplate` signature in `tests/setup.ts`.
>   - Python tests (sync/async): consistently use `.skip_cache()` and/or `skip_cache=True` in build calls across install/run/from/stacktrace suites; add explicit "with skip cache" cases.

title: Bump playwright from 1.48.0 to 1.55.1 in the npm_and_yarn group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the npm_and_yarn group with 1 update in the / directory: [playwright](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright). Updates `playwright` from 1.48.0 to 1.55.1
Release notes

Sourced from playwright's releases.

v1.55.1

Highlights

microsoft/playwright#37479 - [Bug]: Upgrade Chromium to 140.0.7339.186. microsoft/playwright#37147 - [Regression]: Internal error: step id not found. microsoft/playwright#37146 - [Regression]: HTML reporter displays a broken chip link when there are no projects. microsoft/playwright#37137 - Revert "fix(a11y): track inert elements as hidden". microsoft/playwright#37532 - chore: do not use -k option

Browser Versions

  • Chromium 140.0.7339.186
  • Mozilla Firefox 141.0
  • WebKit 26.0

This version was also tested against the following stable channels:

  • Google Chrome 139
  • Microsoft Edge 139

v1.55.0

New APIs

  • New Property testStepInfo.titlePath Returns the full title path starting from the test file, including test and step titles.

Codegen

  • Automatic toBeVisible() assertions: Codegen can now generate automatic toBeVisible() assertions for common UI interactions. This feature can be enabled in the Codegen settings UI.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ Dropped support for Chromium extension manifest v2.

Miscellaneous

  • Added support for Debian 13 "Trixie".

Browser Versions

  • Chromium 140.0.7339.16
  • Mozilla Firefox 141.0
  • WebKit 26.0

This version was also tested against the following stable channels:

  • Microsoft Edge 139

microsoft/playwright#36714 - [Regression]: Codegen is not able to launch in Administrator Terminal on Windows (ProtocolError: Protocol error) microsoft/playwright#36828 - [Regression]: Playwright Codegen keeps spamming with selected option microsoft/playwright#36810 - [Regression]: Starting Codegen with target language doesn't work anymore

title: Expand folder with double star pattern for recursive hash computation
body:
Currently, when you provide a simple folder pattern like `folder/` to copy() it will not check folders contents for changes (only the folder metadata itself) which leads to stale caches.

This fix appends ** pattern when no pattern is specified for recursive hash computation.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Ensure folder COPY patterns hash contents recursively to fix cache invalidation, adding a shared file-gathering helper, tests, and a Python glob dependency.
> 
> - **Template hashing (JS + Python SDKs)**:
>   - Add `getAllFilesForFilesHash` (`packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts`) and `get_all_files_for_files_hash` (`packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py`) to collect directories and nested files using glob patterns and ignores.
>   - Update `calculateFilesHash`/`calculate_files_hash` to use the new helpers, hashing recursive paths, metadata, and file contents; handle symlinks consistently.
> - **Tests**:
>   - Add comprehensive tests for recursive matching, ignore patterns, sorting, empty dirs, symlinks in `packages/js-sdk/tests/.../getAllFilesForFilesHash.test.ts` and Python async/sync tests under `packages/python-sdk/tests/.../test_get_all_files_for_files_hash.py`.
> - **Dependencies**:
>   - Add `wcmatch` in `packages/python-sdk/pyproject.toml` for advanced globbing.
> - **Release**:
>   - Patch bump via `.changeset/green-mice-watch.md`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit d495427d86ad4e4d5a280567baa63df40a3271fb. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: mishushakov

title: Dockerfile output - change ENV var syntax
body:
Current env var syntax won't be anymore supported by Docker and is currently blocking local development.

```
3 warnings found (use docker --debug to expand):
 - LegacyKeyValueFormat: "ENV key=value" should be used instead of legacy "ENV key value" format (line 2)
```

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Use "ENV key=value" in generated Dockerfiles and add tests; minor devcontainer path string cast.
> 
> - **Template/Dockerfile generation**:
>   - **JS SDK (`packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts`)**: Handle `ENV` instructions and output as `ENV key=value ...`.
>   - **Python SDK (`packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py`)**: Same `ENV` formatting; also cast `devcontainer_directory` to `str` in devcontainer start command.
> - **Tests**:
>   - **JS**: Add `toDockerfile` test covering `ENV` output.
>   - **Python (sync/async)**: Add tests for `ENV` output in generated Dockerfiles.
> - **Changeset**: Patch releases for `@e2b/python-sdk` and `e2b`.

title: Update connect method
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> Use new connect API for JS/Python SDKs to connect and only extend (never shorten) running sandbox timeouts, with updated types/docs and tests.
> 
> - **SDKs**:
>   - **JS**:
>     - Replace `resume`/`setTimeout` flow with `SandboxApi.connectSandbox` calling `POST /sandboxes/{sandboxID}/connect`.
>     - Update `Sandbox.connect` (static/instance) to use returned `sandboxDomain`, `envdAccessToken`, `envdVersion`.
>     - Redefine `SandboxConnectOpts` to include `timeoutMs` that only extends existing timeout.
>   - **Python (async/sync)**:
>     - Introduce `_cls_connect` using `post_sandboxes_sandbox_id_connect` with `ConnectSandbox`; return `Sandbox` model.
>     - Update `connect` methods to use API response for domain/token/version; remove extra info fetch.
>     - Docstrings clarify timeout only extends for running sandboxes.
> - **Tests**:
>   - Add cases ensuring connect does not shorten timeout and does extend when longer (JS, async Python, sync Python).
>   - Preserve error on connecting to non-running sandbox.
> - **Meta**:
>   - Changeset: minor releases for `@e2b/python-sdk`, `e2b`, and `@e2b/cli`.

title: Update spec and templates to use /v3/ endpoint
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> Switches SDKs to the /v3 template build API and updates spec with sandbox connect, team max metrics, and related schema changes while deprecating legacy endpoints.
> 
> - **API Spec / Schema**:
>   - **Templates v3**: Add `POST /v3/templates` with `TemplateBuildRequestV3` → `TemplateRequestResponseV3`; deprecate `POST /templates`, `POST /templates/{templateID}`, and `POST /v2/templates` (now return `TemplateLegacy`).
>   - **Sandbox Connect**: Add `POST /sandboxes/{sandboxID}/connect` with `ConnectSandbox`; mark `POST /sandboxes/{sandboxID}/resume` as deprecated.
>   - **Team Metrics**: Add `GET /teams/{teamID}/metrics/max` returning `MaxTeamMetric`.
>   - **Schema updates**: `Template` now includes `buildStatus` (`TemplateBuildStatus`); `BuildLogEntry.step`; `BuildStatusReason.logEntries`; `NodeStatusChange.clusterID`; replace `McpConfig` with nullable `Mcp`; add `timestampUnix` to metrics and deprecate `timestamp`.
> - **JS SDK**:
>   - Update template build request to `POST /v3/templates` in `template/buildApi.ts`.
>   - Regenerate `schema.gen.ts` reflecting new endpoints/types and deprecations.
> - **Python SDK**:
>   - Add client for `POST /v3/templates`; switch sync/async build APIs to use `TemplateBuildRequestV3` and new response type.
>   - Add client for `POST /sandboxes/{sandboxID}/connect`.
>   - Introduce/adjust models: `TemplateBuildStatus`, `TemplateRequestResponseV3`, `TemplateLegacy`, `ConnectSandbox`, `MaxTeamMetric`, `McpType0`, and metric timestamp fields.
> - **Meta**:
>   - Changesets: patch bumps for affected packages.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 4672fbf655ae3e508bf375dc58a89299e19f0664. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: CLI migrate: Rename old template files no longer needed
body:
Here's what it will look like. This highlights that the files are no longer used.

```
🔄 Migrating template configuration to SDK format...

Using language: TypeScript

✅ Generated TypeScript template files:
   ./template.ts
   ./build.dev.ts
   ./build.prod.ts

📁 Old template files no longer needed:
   ./e2b.Dockerfile → ./e2b.Dockerfile.old
   ./e2b.toml → ./e2b.toml.old

🎉 Migration completed successfully!

🔨 You can now build your template using:
   npx tsx build.dev.ts (for development)
   npx tsx build.prod.ts (for production)

Learn more about Template SDK: https://e2b.dev/docs
```



<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

title: Promote beta MCP features to main
body:

title: Add mcp server prepull
body:
Adds prepulling mcp server images to the SDK. For Typescript its fully typed. The tpl name "mcp-gateway-v0-3" will change before the launch

```typescript
export const template = Template()
    .fromTemplate("mcp-gateway")
    .betaAddMcpServer("arxiv")

await Template.build(template, {
    alias: "arxiv-mcp-gateway",
    cpuCount: 1,
    memoryMB: 1024,
    onBuildLogs: console.log,
});

```

```python
sbx = await AsyncSandbox.beta_create(timeout=600, mcp={
    "github/modelcontextprotocol/servers": {

    },
})
```

or in TS with better typing:

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.betaCreate({
    mcp: {
        duckduckgo: {},
        'github/modelcontextprotocol/servers': {
            installCmd: 'npm install',

    },
    timeoutMs: 600_000,
});
```

author: djeebus

reviews.body:
Bug: API URL Ignored in Parameter Handling

 The `api_url` field, though included in `ApiParams`, is silently ignored by `get_api_params` when passed via `opts`. This creates inconsistent behavior and conflicts with the method's docstring, which advises against passing `api_url` to API calls.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L33-L35 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/31ce4970c4ecb00b45a312213b8e2386b66456c0/packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L33-L35
packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L117-L150 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/31ce4970c4ecb00b45a312213b8e2386b66456c0/packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L117-L150

Bug: API URL Handling Inconsistent

 The `get_api_params` method doesn't correctly fall back to `self.api_url` when `api_url` isn't provided in `opts`, unlike other parameters. This can result in `None` being used for the API URL. Additionally, `api_url` is an endpoint URL and shouldn't be included as a parameter in the returned `ApiParams` dictionary.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L152-L153 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0bf0758a619bd1a51319f0da95ac90a907c50b0d/packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L152-L153
packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L120-L127 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0bf0758a619bd1a51319f0da95ac90a907c50b0d/packages/python-sdk/e2b/connection_config.py#L120-L127

title: Fix mcp gateway race condition
body:
Previously the gateway was just fire and forget which lead to a race condition when you immediately tried to access the mcp server, changed the template to launch a daemon with healthcheck.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Start MCP gateway synchronously with error checks and bump default MCP template to `mcp-gateway-v0-2` across JS and Python SDKs.
> 
> - **MCP Gateway startup**:
>   - JS (`packages/js-sdk/src/sandbox/index.ts`): Start `mcp-gateway` without background mode; validate exit code and throw on failure.
>   - Python Async (`packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py`): Await `mcp-gateway` start, remove background/timeout flags; raise on non-zero exit.
>   - Python Sync (`packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py`): Run `mcp-gateway` synchronously; check exit code and raise on failure.
> - **Defaults**:
>   - Update `default_mcp_template` from `mcp-gateway-v0-1` to `mcp-gateway-v0-2` in JS and Python SDKs.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 79e431c78bf384c970a5b1fede55071116a089c5. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

title: Fix e2b tpl publish/delete --config flag
body:
Fixed publish command to honor the `--config` flag by passing it to `getConfigPath`, while preserving default fallback to `e2b.toml`.

Also added test verifying `--config` overrides the default and that the default is used when `--config` isn’t provided.

The setup.ts was configured in a way to run pnpm build in parallel for each test file, which resulted in flaky tests that just now started to fail because there are more test files: https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/actions/runs/18576989022/job/52964215812

title: Fix global packages permissions error
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> Ensure global pip/npm installs run as root; add a pip option to install per-user instead.
> 
> - **SDK Templates**:
>   - **Global installs run as root**:
>     - JS: `pipInstall` and `npmInstall` now execute with `user: 'root'` when installing globally.
>     - Python: `pip_install` and `npm_install` now run commands as `root` when global.
>   - **pip user-level option**:
>     - JS: `pipInstall(packages?, options?: { g?: boolean })` (default `g: true`); adds `--user` and avoids `root` when `g: false`.
>     - Python: `pip_install(packages=None, g=True)`; appends `--user` and avoids `root` when `g` is `False`.
>   - **Docs/types**: Update TypeScript types and docstrings to reflect new pip options and defaults.
> - **Release**:
>   - Bump `@e2b/python-sdk` and `e2b` with patch changeset.

title: feat: keep user and workdir from the template in the sandbox
body:
There is a DX issue with the difference of the default user and working directory between templates and sandboxes (especially visible in the Build System 2.0). The default user and workdir for template is root (/root), but for the sandbox is "user" (/home/user).  This creates an unfortunate hidden difference between the build and sandbox run.


**Current state**

```ts
const template = Template()
  .fromBaseImage()
  .runCmd("whoami") // root
  .runCmd("pwd") // /root

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("whoami") // user
sbx.commands.run("pwd") // /home/user
```

To solve this, we could implement the same logic as Docker uses. The user and workdir would persist from the template to the sandbox. This would mean, that the default user used in the sandbox would be inherited from the last set user in the template. If you don't adjust the user in the template, it will be "root" also in the sandbox. If you adjust it, it can be anything (also, it doesn't have to be "user" at that point).


_Example with root_

```ts
const template = Template()
  .fromBaseImage()
  .runCmd("whoami") // root
  .runCmd("pwd") // /root

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("whoami") // root
sbx.commands.run("pwd") // //root
```

_Example with "user"_

```ts
const template = Template({
  fileContextPath: "myfolder",
})
  .fromBaseImage()
  .setUser("root")
  .runCmd("whoami") // root
  .runCmd("pwd") // /root
  .setUser("user")
  .runCmd("whoami") // user
  .runCmd("pwd") // /home/user

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("whoami") // user
sbx.commands.run("pwd") // /home/user
```

Related infrastructure changes: https://github.com/e2b-dev/infra/pull/1349


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Enable inheriting default user/workdir from templates by allowing omitted `username`, updating SDK auth/signature logic and envd APIs/spec accordingly.
> 
> - **API/Spec (envd)**:
>   - Make `username` optional in `/files` (download/upload) and expand `Metrics` fields.
>   - Add `/init` fields: `defaultUser`, `defaultWorkdir`, `hyperloopIP`, `timestamp`.
> - **JS SDK**:
>   - Generalize `Username` to `string`; introduce version-gated default via `ENVD_DEFAULT_USER`.
>   - Update `authenticationHeader` to accept `envdVersion`, return empty header when user omitted; propagate through `commands`, `pty`, `filesystem`.
>   - File URL/signature: stop always attaching `username`; allow undefined user (with version fallback); adjust signing to handle empty user.
> - **Python SDK**:
>   - Mirror JS changes: `Username = str`, version-aware defaults, optional user in file ops/URLs, updated `authentication_header` and signature handling; thread `envd_version` into commands/pty/filesystem.
> - **Misc**:
>   - Regenerate OpenAPI client schemas; add `ENVD_DEFAULT_USER`; minor formatting/ignore updates.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 5c1e0be05c91774c661315fefe1ecd621c2cf0e0. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Clarifies sandbox 30‑day data retention (based on original creation date, not extended by resumes) and specifies NotFound errors in SDKs.
>

title: fix(types): make stdin optional in CommandOptions
body:
This PR fixes a mismatch between the TypeScript types and the documented/default runtime behavior in the JS SDK.

- The docs indicate `stdin` defaults to `false` (`@default false`).
- The current type declares `stdin: boolean;` (required), which causes TypeScript errors for callers who rely on the default.
- Change: mark `stdin` as optional (`stdin?: boolean;`) so types match docs/runtime.

title: Fix e2b sbx logs -f <id> not following
body:
This PR fixes 2 bugs that made the follow option in `e2b sbx logs` not work:

1. `e2b sbx logs -f <id>` failed to follow because the `isRunning` function was always returning false due to the API call being unauthenticated if you dont have the `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable set.

This follows the existing pattern from other API calls:
https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/c4ce97e9b9a1809e3419d64499c1e56f9138e3d0/packages/cli/src/commands/sandbox/list.ts#L138-L152

Eventually it would make sense to handle the error here, for now just a hotfix to fix the --follow usage

2. Once it actually followed, it never stopped following because `isRunning` was not inside the loop and always true. Now when you kill the sandbox follow actually stops following

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Fixes `e2b sbx logs -f` by authenticating `isRunning` and re-checking sandbox state each loop so following works and stops when closed.
> 
> - **CLI — `packages/cli/src/commands/sandbox`**:
>   - **Logs follow behavior (`logs.ts`)**:
>     - Re-check `isRunning(sandboxID)` inside the loop (`isSandboxRunning`) instead of using a cached promise, ensuring follow stops when sandbox is closed or not found.
>   - **Sandbox state check auth (`utils.ts`)**:
>     - Use `ensureAPIKey()` and pass `apiKey` to `Sandbox.getInfo(getShortID(sandboxID), { apiKey })` so `isRunning` is authenticated.
> - **Release**:
>   - Changeset adds patch for `@e2b/cli` with note: fix sbx logs follow option.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 5fbd87166b70aca7229ccc1ff8fa2c27f12b1641. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Correct file hashing logic in the SDK
body:
- Adds relative path, mode, uid, gid, size, mtimeMs

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Strengthens build cache invalidation by hashing paths and file metadata, adds optional symlink resolution for COPY uploads, updates tar/upload flows, and adds tests for symlink scenarios.
> 
> - **Template hashing & caching**:
>   - JS (`packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts`): `calculateFilesHash` now hashes relative paths and file stats (mode, uid, gid, size, mtime), and handles symlinks (follow or hash link target/path based on `resolveSymlinks`).
>   - Python (`packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py`): mirrors hashing changes and symlink handling.
> - **Symlink resolution option**:
>   - Add `resolveSymlinks` (default `RESOLVE_SYMLINKS = false`) in `consts` (JS/Python).
>   - Extend `Template.copy` and `Instruction`/`CopyItem` types to carry `resolveSymlinks` (JS/Python) and propagate into hashing and uploads.
> - **Archiving & uploads**:
>   - JS: `tarFileStream`/`tarFileStreamUpload` accept `resolveSymlinks` and set tar follow; `uploadFile` takes `resolveSymlinks`.
>   - Python async/sync: `tarfile.open(..., dereference=resolve_symlinks)`; `upload_file` accepts `resolve_symlinks`.
> - **Build pipeline propagation**:
>   - Pass `resolveSymlinks` through build steps for COPY (JS `index.ts`, Python async/sync `main.py`).
>   - Minor type/enum serialization adjustments in Python `_instructions_with_hashes`/`_serialize`.
> - **Tests**:
>   - Add/expand JS and Python tests to cover builds with symlinks and `resolveSymlinks`; introduce build helper fixtures and temp folders; refactor stacktrace tests to use fixtures.
> - **Changeset**: patch bumps for `@e2b/python-sdk` and `e2b` with note: fix template build files copy cache invalidation.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit e0b71b128f7c25eedd25fd64d46e2dc9c7db2a44. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: mishushakov

reviews.body:
Bug: TypedDict Misused as Constructor

 The code attempts to call `CopyItem(...)` as a constructor, but `CopyItem` is a `TypedDict` and cannot be instantiated this way, which causes a `TypeError`. This also results in `copy_items` being assigned a single item instead of the expected `List[CopyItem]`, leading to a runtime error when iterating.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L42-L50 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/4ea959cd815f18670c103a9d9020c932f605f27a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L42-L50

Bug: Fixture Returns Incorrect Type

 The `build` and `async_build` fixtures immediately perform their respective build operations, returning the result. Tests, however, expect these fixtures to return a *callable function* that accepts a template argument, causing runtime errors. The `template: TemplateClass` annotation also incorrectly implies `template` is a fixture parameter.

packages/python-sdk/tests/conftest.py#L66-L84 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/e7681627090c98a1aa62a9bb806ac8e7c7f1f5f2/packages/python-sdk/tests/conftest.py#L66-L84

Bug: Hash Function Ignores Symlinks and Directories

 The `calculateFilesHash` function doesn't account for the `resolveSymlinks` parameter. This causes cache inconsistencies because the hash always processes symlinks as links, while the tar stream may resolve them to their target content. Also, directories are entirely skipped from the hash calculation, so changes to directory structure won't affect the hash, potentially leading to incorrect caching.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L46-L78 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/21ad2ec196771575925f0a6b459a426e35e4a521/packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L46-L78
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L54-L83 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/21ad2ec196771575925f0a6b459a426e35e4a521/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L54-L83
packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L24-L31 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/21ad2ec196771575925f0a6b459a426e35e4a521/packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L24-L31
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L24-L32 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/21ad2ec196771575925f0a6b459a426e35e4a521/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L24-L32
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L669-L680 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/21ad2ec196771575925f0a6b459a426e35e4a521/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L669-L680

Bug: Hash Calculation Ignores Directories

 The `calculateFilesHash` function in both SDKs completely skips directories from hash calculation. This prevents directory relative paths and metadata (like permissions or timestamps) from being included, which can lead to incorrect cache invalidation when directory structures or properties change.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L48-L55 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/facda2a3f60094da157cd40db9f9072212903dd7/packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L48-L55
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L56-L63 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/facda2a3f60094da157cd40db9f9072212903dd7/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L56-L63

Bug: Copy Method Fails with String Source

 The `copy` method incorrectly instantiates `CopyItem` as a class constructor when `src` is a string, causing a `TypeError`. For batch copy operations (when `src` is a list), it uses the method's parameters (`user`, `mode`, etc.) for all items, ignoring their individual `CopyItem` configurations.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L43-L71 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/9f66fbdcf2553b3d824f131ff683597ffd09fc14/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L43-L71
few nits and gtg!

Bug: Async/Sync Path Handling Inconsistency

 The async implementation at line 87 retrieves `resolve_symlinks` without a default, causing it to be `None` when not specified. The sync version explicitly defaults to `False`. This inconsistency means async and sync paths handle symlinks differently, potentially leading to hash mismatches or `None` being passed to boolean parameters.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L86-L87 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/21783d2a26069b43ed5ebc6086542e20b78e8f4a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L86-L87

Bug: Default Parameter Handling Mismatch

 The `resolveSymlinks` parameter is inconsistently handled when omitted from `file_upload` instructions. The Python async SDK passes `None` to `upload_file`, preventing its default from applying. Similarly, the JS SDK passes `undefined` to `tarFileStreamUpload`, bypassing its default. This leads to different behavior between sync and async implementations.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L86-L87 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/805fd29667593f2ad7b66f3193bd1fc8c6e12f20/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L86-L87
packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L99-L104 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/805fd29667593f2ad7b66f3193bd1fc8c6e12f20/packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L99-L104
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L86-L87 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/805fd29667593f2ad7b66f3193bd1fc8c6e12f20/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L86-L87
minor nits, otherwise looks good to go

title: feat: use application/octet-stream for sandbox file uploads
body:
## Summary
- Switches sandbox filesystem file uploads from `multipart/form-data` to `application/octet-stream` in both the JS and Python SDKs
- Each file is now uploaded as raw binary with the path passed as a query parameter, matching the `application/octet-stream` content type in the envd API spec
- Multi-file writes send one request per file sequentially

## Test plan
- [ ] Run JS SDK filesystem write tests (`pnpm run test` in `packages/js-sdk`)
- [ ] Run Python SDK filesystem write tests (`pytest` in `packages/python-sdk`)
- [ ] Verify single file write, multi-file write, and various data types (string, bytes, streams)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: mishushakov

reviews.body:
### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `643b2f61b2`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
Codex has been enabled to automatically review pull requests in this repo. Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. When you [sign up for Codex through ChatGPT](https://openai.com/codex), Codex can also answer questions or update the PR, like "@codex address that feedback".

All previously flagged issues have been addressed in the follow-up commit.


<details>
<summary>Extended reasoning...</summary>

### Overview
This PR switches sandbox filesystem file uploads from `multipart/form-data` to `application/octet-stream` across the JS SDK and both Python SDK variants (sync and async). Each file is now uploaded in a separate POST request with the path passed as a query parameter, matching the envd API spec.

### Previous Review Findings — Now Resolved
My prior review identified three bugs in the original commit (`643b2f6`):
1. **JS SDK empty-array guard** — `if (\!files)` failed to catch a server response of `[]`, silently dropping write results. Fixed in `16ee50d` to `if (\!files || files.length === 0)`.
2. **Python SDK IOBase memory regression** — `file_data.read()` buffered the entire stream into memory before uploading. Fixed in `16ee50d` by passing `content=file_data` directly, restoring lazy streaming.
3. **JS SDK AbortSignal timeout bug** — multi-file write path used `opts?.requestTimeoutMs` (always `undefined`) instead of `writeOpts?.requestTimeoutMs`. Fixed in `16ee50d`.

All three inline comments are marked resolved. The current HEAD (`16ee50d`) contains all the fixes.

### Security Risks
None. This is a sandbox file upload path (not auth, crypto, or permissions). The change only affects the wire format and request structure for file writes within an isolated sandbox.

### Level of Scrutiny
Moderate. Changing the upload format is a meaningful behavioral change, but the implementation is now correct and consistent across all three SDK files. The fix commit directly addressed the review feedback with minimal, targeted changes.

### Other Factors
The bug hunting system found no new issues in the current state. The test plan in the PR description covers the relevant scenarios (single file, multi-file, various data types). Missing changeset is a process concern for the maintainer to handle, not a code correctness issue.

</details>

commits.message:
feat: use application/octet-stream for file uploads instead of multipart/form-data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address review feedback for octet-stream upload

- JS SDK: check for empty array in response validation (!files || files.length === 0)
- JS SDK: use writeOpts?.requestTimeoutMs instead of opts? for multi-file writes
- Python SDK: stream IOBase directly instead of .read() to avoid buffering large files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: version-gate octet-stream upload, fall back to multipart for older envd

Adds ENVD_OCTET_STREAM_UPLOAD version constant (0.6.0). When envd
supports it, uploads use application/octet-stream per-file. For older
envd versions, falls back to the original multipart/form-data approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set ENVD_OCTET_STREAM_UPLOAD version to 0.5.7

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: use concurrent uploads for multi-file octet-stream writes

The octet-stream path uploaded files sequentially (N requests in series),
unlike the old multipart path which batched in 1 request. Use Promise.all
in JS and asyncio.gather in Python async to upload concurrently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: read IOBase streams into bytes for octet-stream uploads

Passing raw IOBase (e.g. BytesIO) directly as content= to httpx async
client causes "Attempted to send a sync request with an AsyncClient"
because httpx expects bytes, not a sync stream. Call .read() to
materialize the stream content first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: read file data into memory before concurrent uploads

Separate the data-reading phase from the upload phase so streams
(BytesIO, StringIO, etc.) are fully consumed into bytes before
concurrent uploads begin. Fixes async httpx failing on sync stream
objects passed as content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revert "fix: read file data into memory before concurrent uploads"

This reverts commit 3ef5ef78c2f42b5c1e38976e1cf001d9be13c7a3.

title: fix: add queue for terminal input
body:
This PR adds a queue for the terminal input. This should help when network delay is large to prevent mangling characters order.

I couldn't use the streamed input, because we need to support browser in our SDK.


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Queues and batches stdin to the sandbox PTY (flushed every 10ms) to preserve input order under network latency, with proper start/stop cleanup.
> 
> - **CLI Terminal (`packages/cli/src/terminal.ts`)**:
>   - Introduce `BatchedQueue` to batch stdin `Buffer`s and send combined input to `sandbox.pty.sendInput`.
>   - Add `FLUSH_INPUT_INTERVAL_MS = 10` and use queued flushing (`start`/`stop`) around session lifecycle.
>   - Replace direct stdin handler with queued `inputQueue.push(data)`; ensure cleanup awaits `inputQueue.stop()`.
>   - Add error handling in queue flush to log send failures.
>

title: Set stdin to `/dev/null` by default
body:
Disable stdin (setting it to `/dev/null` for `command.run()`, but enable to setting it to pipe, which you can send the input via `command.sendStdin()`
This fixes issues with tools checking for stdin and then hanging indefinitely

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Disables stdin by default and introduces a new `StartRequest.stdin` flag to opt-in to stdin piping, updating JS/Python SDKs, version gating, and tests.
> 
> - **Spec/Protobuf**:
>   - Add `optional bool stdin` to `process.StartRequest` in `spec/envd/process/process.proto` and regenerate JS/Python protos.
> - **JS SDK**:
>   - Add `ENVD_COMMANDS_STDIN = '0.3.0'` and version check; error if `stdin === false` on older envd.
>   - Extend `CommandStartOpts` with `stdin` (default `false`); pass `stdin` to `rpc.start`.
> - **Python SDK**:
>   - Add `ENVD_COMMANDS_STDIN = Version("0.3.0")` and version check mirroring JS.
>   - Extend async/sync `commands.run()` with `stdin` (default `False`); pass to `StartRequest`.
>   - Update generated `process_pb2`/`.pyi` with `StartRequest.stdin`.
> - **Tests**:
>   - Update send-stdin tests to run commands with `stdin: true`/`stdin=True`.
> - **Changeset**:
>   - Minor bump for `@e2b/python-sdk` and `e2b`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 458a15187e6eadccd43540e6b8972c4d45e1bb78. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: jakubno

reviews.body:
Bug: Version Check Fails with None and Incorrectly for Older Versions

 The version check condition in the `_start` method has an operator precedence issue. This causes a `TypeError` when `self._envd_version` is `None` and `stdin` is `True`. It also incorrectly raises an exception for older sandbox versions, even when `stdin` is `True`, because the version check isn't properly gated by the `stdin is False` condition.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/commands/command.py#L239-L248 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/373837e92cceffed8ab0d0ee3f0446b0ecb96bb6/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/commands/command.py#L239-L248
packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/commands/command.py#L239-L248 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/373837e92cceffed8ab0d0ee3f0446b0ecb96bb6/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/commands/command.py#L239-L248

Bug: TypeScript Error: `stdin` Property Inconsistency

 The `stdin` property in `CommandStartOpts` is defined as required (`stdin: boolean`). This is a breaking change for existing code and creates a type inconsistency, as the implementation treats `stdin` as optional with a default (`opts?.stdin || false`), leading to TypeScript compilation errors.

packages/js-sdk/src/sandbox/commands/index.ts#L73-L74 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/e11b1d28635a3f687626d4492375ee5bebfd45bb/packages/js-sdk/src/sandbox/commands/index.ts#L73-L74
one nit

Bug: Version Check Bypass for `stdin` Parameter

 The `stdin` parameter's version check in `run` happens before its default `False` value is applied. If `stdin` is `None`, the check is bypassed, but `stdin` then defaults to `False`. This allows `stdin=False` to be sent to older `envd` versions that don't support it, without raising the expected `SandboxException`.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/commands/command.py#L212-L221 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/458a15187e6eadccd43540e6b8972c4d45e1bb78/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/commands/command.py#L212-L221
packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/commands/command.py#L209-L218 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/458a15187e6eadccd43540e6b8972c4d45e1bb78/packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/commands/command.py#L209-L218

title: feat: add template init for new SDK
body:
## 🚀 Add template init for new SDK

This PR updates the `e2b template init-v2` (or `e2b template create`) command to leverage the new E2B Templates SDK, providing a more streamlined and developer-friendly experience for creating sandbox templates.

The command will be migrated to just `init` once the Template SDK becomes the default to create new templates.

### 🔧 Key Features

#### **Template Structure**
```
my-template/
├── README.md            # Information about the template
├── template.ts          # Template definition using E2B SDK
├── build.dev.ts         # Development build script
└── build.prod.ts        # Production build script
```

#### **Generated Build Scripts**
- `npm run e2b:build:dev` - Build development template
- `npm run e2b:build:prod` - Build production template

#### **Template Definition Example**
```typescript
const template = Template()
  .fromImage('ubuntu:22.04')
  .runCmd('echo Hello World E2B!')
```
author: dobrac

 The `generateTypeScriptCode` and `generatePythonCode` functions incorrectly resolve Handlebars template paths. `path.join(__dirname, 'templates')` points to `dist/commands/template/generators/templates/` instead of the expected `dist/templates/`, leading to template files not being found at runtime.

Mostly minor nits, the biggest change is to change package.json to Makefile for Python and create a new one when one does not exist already. In the CLI please do not throw on validation, exit the process with console.error and a error code instead

 Commander.js option validators exit the process directly instead of throwing an error. This bypasses Commander's error handling, preventing proper help/usage display and complicating testing.

 The `migrate` command's `--language` option parser now uses `console.error` and `process.exit(1)` for invalid input. This bypasses Commander.js's error handling, leading to abrupt termination and inconsistent error messages compared to other validations.

packages/cli/src/commands/template/migrate.ts#L101-L108 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/2071cf02775ef8ac155776eb71e2e1748fbb5a03/packages/cli/src/commands/template/migrate.ts#L101-L108

 The `init-v2` command generates templates using `Template().fromBaseImage()` without arguments. This doesn't align with the `Template().fromImage('ubuntu:22.04')` pattern expected by tests and shown in README examples, meaning generated templates might not use the intended base image.

title: feat: add template migration to CLI
body:
## 🔄 Add Template Migration to CLI

### Summary

This PR introduces a new `migrate` command to the E2B CLI that helps users transition from the legacy Dockerfile + TOML configuration format to the new Template SDK format. The migration tool automatically converts existing `e2b.Dockerfile` and `e2b.toml` configurations into SDK-compatible template files using Handlebars templates.

### What's Changed

#### ✨ New Features

- **Template Migration Command**: Added `e2b template migrate` command for converting legacy configurations to SDK format
- **Multi-Language Support**: Supports migration to TypeScript, Python (sync), and Python (async) formats
- **Interactive Language Selection**: CLI prompts for target language when not specified
- **Multi-stage Dockerfile Support**: Added fallback handling for complex multi-stage Dockerfiles

#### 🔧 Technical Improvements

- **Enhanced ENV Handling**: Improved parsing of multiple environment variables from single ENV instructions
- **File Name Conflict Resolution**: Automatic unique filename generation to prevent overwriting existing files
- **Comprehensive Template Generation**: Creates both development and production build files

#### 📁 New Template Files

- `typescript-template.hbs` - TypeScript template generation
- `typescript-build.hbs` - TypeScript build script template
- `python-template.hbs` - Python template generation (supports both sync/async)
- `python-build-async.hbs` - Python async build script template
- `python-build-sync.hbs` - Python sync build script template

### Usage

```bash
# Migrate with interactive language selection
e2b template migrate

# Migrate to specific language
e2b template migrate --language typescript
e2b template migrate --language python-sync
e2b template migrate --language python-async

# Custom paths
e2b template migrate --dockerfile custom.dockerfile --config custom.toml
```

### Generated Output

The migration command generates three files per target:
- `template.{ts|py}` - Template definition using SDK
- `build{.|_}dev.{ts|py}` - Development build script
- `build{.|_}prod.{ts|py}` - Production build script

### Migration Process

1. **Parse Configuration**: Reads `e2b.toml` for template settings
2. **Dockerfile Analysis**: Uses E2B SDK to parse Dockerfile instructions
3. **Transformation**: Converts Docker instructions to SDK method calls
4. **Code Generation**: Uses Handlebars templates to generate target language files
5. **File Creation**: Writes template and build files with conflict resolution

### Error Handling

- **Graceful Dockerfile Parsing**: Falls back to custom image reference for unparseable Dockerfiles
- **Clear User Guidance**: Provides manual build instructions when automatic conversion fails
- **Validation**: Ensures required configuration files exist before migration
author: dobrac

reviews.body:
Bug: Circular Dependency in Dockerfile Parsing

 This change introduces a circular dependency between `dockerfileParser.ts` and `index.ts`. `dockerfileParser.ts` now imports `waitForTimeout` from `index.ts`, but `index.ts` also imports `parseDockerfile` from `dockerfileParser.ts`. This reintroduces a circular dependency that was previously avoided by an inline implementation, potentially causing module loading issues or runtime errors.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L7-L9 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/51f82108d66bda67a9e3bc2c0ebae7e5adfedaa6/packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L7-L9

Bug: Import Error and Circular Dependency

 The `waitForTimeout` function is incorrectly imported from `./index` in `dockerfileParser.ts`. This causes a compilation error because `waitForTimeout` is not exported from that file. Additionally, this import introduces a circular dependency, as `./index` itself imports `dockerfileParser.ts`, which the previous inline implementation explicitly avoided.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L7-L9 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/751f502a072f704d6a03dee53e4421d77b8a8824/packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L7-L9

Bug: Help Text Displays Indices Instead of Options

 The help text for the `--language` option uses `Object.keys()` on the `validLanguages` array, which causes it to display array indices instead of the actual language options. This makes the command's help message less informative for users.

packages/cli/src/commands/template/migrate.ts#L370-L374 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/f11e9d7102212a8f38c8bbf03dc9a9ea61022549/packages/cli/src/commands/template/migrate.ts#L370-L374
minor nits, otherwise looks good to me :)

Bug: Python Sync Case Fails Through to Default

 The `case (Language.PythonSync, Language.PythonAsync):` statement uses the comma operator, which evaluates to `Language.PythonAsync`. This means `Language.PythonSync` will incorrectly fall through to the default case, causing an "Unsupported language" error when generating synchronous Python templates.

packages/cli/src/commands/template/generators/template-generator.ts#L54-L55 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/5edd8704d4431826f4fe17d21398966795f9a6bb/packages/cli/src/commands/template/generators/template-generator.ts#L54-L55

Bug: Python Sync Template Matching Fails

 The `case (Language.PythonSync, Language.PythonAsync):` statement incorrectly uses the comma operator. This evaluates only to `Language.PythonAsync`, causing `Language.PythonSync` to never match. As a result, synchronous Python templates fall through to the default case, throwing an "Unsupported language" error.

packages/cli/src/commands/template/generators/template-generator.ts#L54-L55 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/e13f840833a9bdaef8155547c80d693f21387b3a/packages/cli/src/commands/template/generators/template-generator.ts#L54-L55
all looks good, just some minor comment

Bug: Global State Causes Handlebars Helper Registration Issues

 The `helpersRegistered` variable is a module-level global instead of a class property, as discussed in PR feedback. This creates a shared state issue where only the first `Handlebars` instance registers helpers, causing subsequent instances to skip registration and potentially miss helpers.

packages/cli/src/commands/template/generators/handlebars.ts#L7-L38 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/a58c2c78294115a6fb9b9e5811f52afc2d1664e7/packages/cli/src/commands/template/generators/handlebars.ts#L7-L38

title: Render failed layer stack traces on build error
body:
### Changelog

- Added traces for Python and JavaScript SDK.
- Added methods for stack trace collection, stack trace context
- Updated file upload mechanism for tracing
- Changed API handling to support throwing API errors with traces

> **Note**
> For functions like `aptInstall`, `fromRegistry` which call `.run` and `.fromImage`, the stack trace has to be replaced so it throws correctly from parent.

> **Note**
> There are no build logs in the trace, we can add this at later point.

### Examples

Keep in mind that the way the traces are rendered entirely depends on environment (Bun, Node, Deno, Vitest, Pytest)
