reviews.body:
Bug: Traceback Creation Error

 The `capture_stack_trace()` function attempts to directly instantiate a `TracebackType` object. However, `TracebackType` is a type annotation, not a constructor, and Python only creates traceback objects during exception handling. This will cause a `TypeError` at runtime.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L98-L112 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/c967b9454e1c7e0c1fe29c9e0b862ce2120ea2d6/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L98-L112
can you please add few edge cases tests for the captured stacktraces so we know they work correctly?

Bug: Array Access Without Bounds Check

 The `replaceLastStackTrace` method accesses `this.stackTraces[this.stackTraces.length - 1]` without checking if the array is empty. If empty, this creates a property `"-1"` on the array object rather than modifying an element. This missing bounds check, unlike its Python counterpart, could lead to silent bugs.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L492-L495 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8f448ddf305af4ff66266e38e184570ca5fd90f4/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L492-L495

Bug: Unsupported Manual Traceback Construction

 The `capture_stack_trace()` function attempts to manually construct a `TracebackType` object. Python's `TracebackType` is an internal type created by the runtime during exception handling, not designed for direct manual instantiation. This approach is not supported and will likely result in a `TypeError` or an invalid traceback object, causing the stack trace feature to malfunction.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L98-L112 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/04ee1f1aff4cb3ee653193b1f3501da50ca70d94/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L98-L112

Bug: Mutable Default Argument Causes Stale Stack Traces

 The `stack_traces` parameter in `wait_for_build_finish` uses a mutable default argument (`[]`). This causes the same list object to be reused across calls, which can lead to stack traces from previous builds incorrectly appearing in subsequent error reports.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L138-L139 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/d3d6d1c1d405b6e3848c0ac407267e1102fa8728/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L138-L139
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L138-L139 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/d3d6d1c1d405b6e3848c0ac407267e1102fa8728/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L138-L139

Bug: Mutable Default Argument Causes Error Leakage

 The `stack_traces` parameter in `wait_for_build_finish` (both async and sync versions) uses a mutable default argument. This creates a shared list object that persists across function calls, which can lead to stack traces from previous calls leaking into new calls and causing incorrect error reporting.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L138-L139 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/7b0e00c49e7b95c89269a7e82ab36851986cbb8b/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L138-L139
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L138-L139 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/7b0e00c49e7b95c89269a7e82ab36851986cbb8b/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L138-L139

Bug: Duplicate Stack Traces in Builder Methods

 Several builder methods in the Python SDK collect duplicate stack traces. This occurs when a method calls another method (like `run_cmd` or `from_image`) that already appends a stack trace, and then the calling method also appends one. This misalignment between instructions and stack traces can lead to incorrect error mapping during build failures.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L165-L168 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/fb5a62816754db8fd2b90fdf504e3c8bb064616a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L165-L168
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L366-L378 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/fb5a62816754db8fd2b90fdf504e3c8bb064616a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L366-L378
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L386-L415 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/fb5a62816754db8fd2b90fdf504e3c8bb064616a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L386-L415
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L183-L200 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/fb5a62816754db8fd2b90fdf504e3c8bb064616a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L183-L200
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L214-L217 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/fb5a62816754db8fd2b90fdf504e3c8bb064616a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L214-L217
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L370-L372 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/fb5a62816754db8fd2b90fdf504e3c8bb064616a/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L370-L372
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L80-L82 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/fb5a62816754db8f

Bug: Duplicate Stack Traces in Template Methods

 Several `TemplateBuilder` and `TemplateBase` methods are collecting duplicate stack traces. This happens when a method calls another internal method (like `run_cmd` or `from_image`) that already appends a stack trace, and then the calling method immediately appends another. This leads to misaligned stack traces, making it harder to map build errors to the correct source code.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L182-L200 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0cc0f7711e90869834f3fad692e1deda7a91fdfd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L182-L200
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L386-L414 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0cc0f7711e90869834f3fad692e1deda7a91fdfd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L386-L414
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L164-L168 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0cc0f7711e90869834f3fad692e1deda7a91fdfd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L164-L168
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L366-L378 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0cc0f7711e90869834f3fad692e1deda7a91fdfd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L366-L378
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L213-L217 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0cc0f7711e90869834f3fad692e1deda7a91fdfd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L213-L217
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L102-L111 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0cc0f7711e90869834f3fad692e1deda7a91fdfd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L102-L111
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L79-L82 https://github.co

Bug: Duplicate Stack Traces in File Operations

 Methods like `remove`, `rename`, `make_dir`, and `make_symlink` collect duplicate stack traces. They call `run_cmd()`, which already collects a trace, then immediately call `self._template._collect_stack_trace()` again. This results in two traces per operation, potentially causing incorrect indexing when matching traces to build steps.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L70-L111 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8351c8823cd8a9a38d23f6b55318e07dad7c2c55/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L70-L111

Bug: TracebackType Instantiation Forbidden

 Direct instantiation of TracebackType is not supported in Python. TracebackType objects are meant to be created by the Python runtime, not manually constructed. This will likely cause runtime errors or unexpected behavior.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L98-L112 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/75ed0cdbda5770949caa3e567eb28ef4687a7f85/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L98-L112

Bug: Mutable Default Argument Causes Shared State

 The `wait_for_build_finish` function uses a mutable default argument for `stack_traces`. This creates a shared list instance across function calls, which can lead to unexpected behavior when the list is modified.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L138-L139 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0bec5f17a66b43cc5067dbc045b08ca901a892cf/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L138-L139
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L138-L139 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0bec5f17a66b43cc5067dbc045b08ca901a892cf/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L138-L139

Bug: File Uploads Fail Silently Without Error Handling

 The `uploadFile` function no longer checks the `fetch` response's `ok` status. Since `fetch` doesn't throw for HTTP error codes (4xx/5xx), failed uploads will silently succeed, leading to undetected file upload failures. This also results in less specific error messages.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L96-L116 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/4a9130ab4eb797fab1343aa83440d8bc3c76036f/packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L96-L116

Bug: Duplicate File Upload Logic Causes Redundancy

 The file upload loop contains duplicated logic, which processes each file twice and can lead to redundant uploads. This also causes the "All file uploads completed" message to log for every file instead of once after all uploads are complete.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L94-L149 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/0465c37588b989e300b421343308201f3915a7bd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L94-L149

Bug: Inefficient Array Search by Index

 Using `Array.prototype.find()` to retrieve an element by a known index is inefficient. Direct array access (`array[index]`) is more performant and clearer, as `find()` unnecessarily iterates through the array to match the index. This pattern appears in `buildApi.ts` and `index.ts`.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L236-L237 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8edc4fea64373efb0f19c92a0b0fb781b5f6b3b9/packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L236-L237
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L573-L574 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8edc4fea64373efb0f19c92a0b0fb781b5f6b3b9/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L573-L574
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L649-L650 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8edc4fea64373efb0f19c92a0b0fb781b5f6b3b9/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L649-L650

Bug: Inconsistent Type Handling in Build Step Functions

 The `getBuildStepIndex` (JS) and `get_build_step_index` (Python) functions don't gracefully handle non-numeric `step` values. This can lead to `NaN` (JS) or `ValueError` (Python) when converting `step` to a number, potentially disrupting stack trace retrieval. Additionally, the JS function uses inconsistent equality operators (`==` vs `===`) for string comparisons, which may introduce type coercion issues.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L133-L147 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/2305da0cef9e3bcf69e191e3bfad5f55ae470ad6/packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L133-L147
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L115-L124 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/2305da0cef9e3bcf69e191e3bfad5f55ae470ad6/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L115-L124

Bug: Stack Trace Indexing Flaw

 The JS SDK has an off-by-one error when indexing stack traces for `COPY` instructions. Using `index + 1` skips the first collected stack trace and can lead to out-of-bounds access for the last instruction. Unlike the Python SDK, the JS SDK lacks bounds checking for this array access.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L573-L574 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8d0b7fe6b1c27554c654138623c3fc230cc88975/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L573-L574
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L649-L650 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8d0b7fe6b1c27554c654138623c3fc230cc88975/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L649-L650
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L86-L89 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8d0b7fe6b1c27554c654138623c3fc230cc88975/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L86-L89
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L86-L89 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8d0b7fe6b1c27554c654138623c3fc230cc88975/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L86-L89
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L499-L502 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8d0b7fe6b1c27554c654138623c3fc230cc88975/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L499-L502
looks great! just few nits

Bug: Loose Equality Causes Unexpected Type Coercion

 The `getBuildStepIndex` function uses loose equality (`==`) for string comparison in the `if (step == 'finalize')` condition. This is inconsistent with strict equality (`===`) used elsewhere in the function and can lead to unexpected type coercion.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L141-L142 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/918e8623776d3ff25a73fb263c13ae5be18cd66f/packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L141-L142

Bug: Unprotected Array Access in Code

 The code accesses `this.stackTraces[index + 1]` without a bounds check. This creates a risk of out-of-bounds array access, which could lead to runtime errors. The Python equivalent includes a bounds check for this scenario.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L574-L575 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/06e4d7c272175f3d5fa6e900612f179619168327/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L574-L575
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L650-L651 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/06e4d7c272175f3d5fa6e900612f179619168327/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L650-L651

Bug: Stack Trace Indexing Error

 The `stackTraces` array is accessed using `index + 1` when processing build instructions. This indexing is fragile because the number of collected stack traces might not consistently align with the number of build instructions. Without bounds checking, this could lead to incorrect or missing stack traces associated with build steps.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L578-L579 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/4d977570182eaded2bb627dc43ebe5a1a8d8f5f6/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L578-L579
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L660-L661 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/4d977570182eaded2bb627dc43ebe5a1a8d8f5f6/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L660-L661

Bug: Enum Type Mismatch in Instruction Handling

 The `Instruction` TypedDict expects an `InstructionType` enum for its `type` field. However, in `_instructions_with_hashes`, the `type` is assigned as a string by accessing `instruction["type"].value`, leading to a type mismatch and potential type checking errors.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L506-L513 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/dec0b39cf51eabea0fb02758cf063902b39b3ace/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L506-L513

Bug: Traceback Loss When Passing None

 Calling `exception.with_traceback(stack_trace)` when `stack_trace` is `None` clears the exception's existing traceback instead of preserving it. This pattern appears in multiple locations, potentially leading to a loss of valuable debugging information.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L52-L53 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/17a680f54c49ba36ec92754a030b8b789bfa503f/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L52-L53
packages/python-sdk/e2b/api/__init__.py#L52-L58 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/17a680f54c49ba36ec92754a030b8b789bfa503f/packages/python-sdk/e2b/api/__init__.py#L52-L58
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L70-L78 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/17a680f54c49ba36ec92754a030b8b789bfa503f/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L70-L78
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L70-L78 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/17a680f54c49ba36ec92754a030b8b789bfa503f/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L70-L78

title: Added format option to CLI sandbox + templates list
body: This PR is based on https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/pull/902, please proceed with that first **Changelog** - Added `--format` option to "e2b sandbox list" and "e2b template list". Can either be "table" or "json" Sandboxes list in JSON format Templates list in JSON format

reviews.body:
Bug: Column Definitions Mismatch SandboxInfo Fields

 The `renderTable` function's column definitions use `name: 'name'` and `name: 'templateId'`. These property names don't align with the `SandboxInfo` object's actual fields, `alias` and `templateID`. As a result, the 'Alias' and 'Template ID' columns will display `undefined` values in the table output.

packages/cli/src/commands/sandbox/list.ts#L70-L76 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/1a194bf097afd00d31097c99acd417fcac280808/packages/cli/src/commands/sandbox/list.ts#L70-L76

Bug: Inconsistent Error Handling Across CLI Commands

 The `template list` command handles unsupported output formats inconsistently. When an invalid format is provided, it uses `return console.error(...)`, causing the process to exit with a success status (0) instead of an error code. This differs from other CLI commands, like `sandbox list`, which terminate the process with `process.exit(1)` on error.

packages/cli/src/commands/template/list.ts#L41-L42 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/554c317670d9be105c9265dcba6e5dcd9599eb56/packages/cli/src/commands/template/list.ts#L41-L42
There also extra whitespace for templates in json

reviews.body:
Bug: Filter Options Ignored Without `--all`

 The `--state` and `--metadata` filter options are silently ignored when used without the `--all` flag, leading to unexpected behavior. Separately, console messages for `--all` operations always refer to 'running sandboxes', even when different states are specified via the `--state` filter, making the output misleading.

packages/cli/src/commands/sandbox/kill.ts#L31-L103 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/caa6b6561fd263504e64841dc22525e976e1a6b9/packages/cli/src/commands/sandbox/kill.ts#L31-L103
Nice!

title: Add custom registries support for pulling base images
body:
### Changelog

- added support for pulling base images from AWS, Docker, GCP registries.
- removed unnecessary conversion in build_api.py.
- synced serialize implementation in TypeScript and Python.
- added Cursor Pylance config (side-effect, can delete if unwanted).

### Examples

**TypeScript**

```ts
import { Template } from 'e2b'

// Generic Docker registry
Template().fromRegistry('ubuntu:22.04', {
  username: 'jirka',
  password: 'password',
})

// AWS ECR registry
Template().fromAWSRegistry('ubuntu:22.04', {
  accessKeyId: '123',
  secretAccessKey: '456',
  region: 'eu-west-1',
})

// GCP GCR registry from file path
Template().fromGCPRegistry('ubuntu:22.04', {
  serviceAccountJSON: './service_account.json',
})

// GCP GCR registry from object
Template().fromGCPRegistry('ubuntu:22.04', {
  serviceAccountJSON: { project_id: '123', private_key_id: '456' },
})
```

**Python**

```py
from e2b import Template

# Generic Docker registry
Template().from_registry(
    image="ubuntu:22.04",
    username="jirka",
    password="password",
)

# AWS ECR registry
Template().from_aws_registry(
    image="ubuntu:22.04",
    access_key_id="123",
    secret_access_key="456",
    region="eu-west-1",
)

# GCP GCR registry from file path
Template().from_gcp_registry(
    image="ubuntu:22.04",
    service_account_json="./service_account.json",
)

# GCP GCR registry from object
Template().from_gcp_registry(
    image="ubuntu:22.04",
    service_account_json={"project_id": "123", "private_key_id": "456"},
)
```
author: mishushakov

reviews.body:
Bug: Template Validation Removed in SDKs

 The `serialize` methods in both the JavaScript and Python SDKs no longer validate that a template specifies either a base image or a base template. This removes client-side validation, allowing invalid templates to be sent to the API, which can lead to build failures or unclear server-side errors.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L610-L632 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/74ffd267b0af29181332386ee0115173c5ce8c75/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L610-L632
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L94-L96 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/74ffd267b0af29181332386ee0115173c5ce8c75/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L94-L96
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L437-L458 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/74ffd267b0af29181332386ee0115173c5ce8c75/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L437-L458

Bug: Registry Config Persistence Bug

 The `registryConfig` isn't cleared when changing the template's base source. If a registry configuration was previously set, it persists when calling `fromImage` without registry options, `fromTemplate`, or `fromDockerfile`. This can lead to `registryConfig` being incorrectly serialized alongside other base source definitions.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L105-L156 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/535870f08da4c413e77b50aa1c3a3868fd1911d2/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L105-L156
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L292-L337 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/535870f08da4c413e77b50aa1c3a3868fd1911d2/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L292-L337

Bug: Registry Config Persists Across Image Sources

 The `registryConfig` field isn't reset when changing the base image source. If a registry configuration was previously set (e.g., via `fromRegistry`), it persists when calling `fromImage` without a config, `fromTemplate`, or `fromDockerfile`. This can lead to unintended authentication or incorrect build data serialization.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L105-L156 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/ef5de4c363eb34dca7e814d644e1fc3c91b8c449/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L105-L156
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L308-L337 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/ef5de4c363eb34dca7e814d644e1fc3c91b8c449/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L308-L337

title: Vendor strip-ansi npm module to avoid ESM-related issues
body:
We bundled `strip-ansi` module but it is ESM-only, it caused a runtime when trying to `require()` ESM-only module from the bundle (on older Node versions).

- Inlined the code from `strip-ansi` into `utils.ts` so it can be correctly bundled.
- Fixes #894

title: Add Templates SDK
body:
### Merge Templates SDK into E2B SDK.

This PR merges code from Templates SDK to E2B SDK.

**Changelog**

- Added new error classes: BuildError, FileUploadError.
- Added template API endpoints to API Client generation (Python).
- Updated API Clients error handling to throw BuildError in addition to SandboxError.
- Exported Template and TemplateAsync classes.
- Moved `getRuntime` utility method from api to utils.
- Browser is supported except file operations (COPY). Incompatible modules are not bundled, using dynamic imports (tar, glob) in supported environments (Node, Deno, Bun) instead.
- Basic tests were added, subject to expansion in a follow-up PR.
- Breaking: Before, 401 error was raising `SandboxError`, which was incorrect, now it will raise `AuthenticationError`. I have also changed the `AuthenticationError` to extend Error class, not `SandboxError` anymore as it's now thrown by both Sandbox and Template.

**Usage**

JavaScript

```js
import { Template } from 'e2b';

const template = Template()
  .fromImage('ubuntu:22.04')
  .copy('folder/*.txt', 'folder', { forceUpload: true })
  .setEnvs({
    ENV_1: 'value1',
    ENV_2: 'value2',
  })
  .runCmd('cat folder/test.txt')
  .setWorkdir('/app')
  .setStartCmd('echo "Hello, world!"', Template.waitForTimeout('10s'))

await Template.build(template, {
  alias: 'test-template',
  cpuCount: 1,
  memoryMB: 1024,
  onBuildLogs: (logEntry) => console.log(logEntry.toString()),
})

```

Python

```py
from e2b import Template

template = (
    Template()
    .from_image("ubuntu:22.04")
    .copy("folder/*.txt", "folder", force_upload=True)
    .set_envs(
        {
            "ENV_1": "value1",
            "ENV_2": "value2",
        }
    )
    .run_cmd("cat folder/test.txt")
    .set_workdir("/app")
    .set_start_cmd("echo 'Hello, world!'", Template.wait_for_timeout("10s"))
)

Template.build(
    template,
    alias="test-template",
    cpu_count=1,
    memory_mb=1024,
    on_build_logs=lambda log_entry: print(log_entry),
)
```

Python Async

```py
from e2b import AsyncTemplate

template = (
    AsyncTemplate()
    .from_image("ubuntu:22.04")
    .copy("folder/*.txt", "folder", force_upload=True)
    .set_envs(
        {
            "ENV_1": "value1",
            "ENV_2": "value2",
        }
    )
    .run_cmd("cat folder/test.txt")
    .set_workdir("/app")
    .set_start_cmd("echo 'Hello, world!'", Template.wait_for_timeout("10s"))
)

await AsyncTemplate.build(
    template,
    alias="test-template",
    cpu_count=1,
    memory_mb=1024,
    on_build_logs=lambda log_entry: print(log_entry),
)
```

---

Merge progress

- [x] JavaScript SDK
- [x] Python SDK
- [x] Tests
- [x] Split files for review
author: mishushakov

reviews.body:
Bug: API Error Handling Fails on Missing Message

 The 401 error handling in `handle_api_exception` directly accesses `body['message']`, which can raise a `KeyError` if the key is missing. This also leads to duplicate message text if the key exists, as it's appended again after an existence check.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/api/__init__.py#L36-L42 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/640c1a61a7358de1f1adbeb80091933295f722cd/packages/python-sdk/e2b/api/__init__.py#L36-L42
### ✅ Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no bugs!

Bug: Build Log Offset Calculation Error

 In `__wait_for_build_finish` (both sync and async), `logs_offset` increments using `len(build_status.logs)` while log iteration uses `build_status.log_entries`. This inconsistent field usage can lead to incorrect offset calculations and potentially missing or duplicate build log entries.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L169-L178 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/005ae1284f2d6b24c728ff1da066d414f1091fff/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L169-L178
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L175-L178 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/005ae1284f2d6b24c728ff1da066d414f1091fff/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L175-L178

\+ the Bugbot comment

Also, did you check the SDK reference for now?


\+ we should add proper documentation comments to all methods. We can do it in subsequent PR

Bug: Inconsistent Exception Handling in Sync/Async Methods

 The `get_file_upload_link` function in the sync version raises `BuildException` for HTTP errors, which differs from the `FileUploadException` raised by its async equivalent. For consistency and clarity, `FileUploadException` would be more fitting for this file upload related operation.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L61-L63 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/7c2035e6b089bad20822e24354829e5efdec98d4/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L61-L63

Bug: Mutable Defaults Cause Instance State Sharing

 The `TemplateBase` class and its `__init__` method use mutable default values for several class variables (e.g., `_instructions`, `_base_image`) and the `ignore_file_paths` parameter. This causes state to be shared across all `TemplateBase` instances, leading to unexpected behavior when one instance's modifications affect others.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L236-L252 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/8be695bb749ea61c8fd2a6299a6a30334b6ce23b/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L236-L252

Bug: Shared State in TemplateBase Causes Interference

 The `TemplateBase` class uses class variables for instance-specific state (e.g., `_instructions`, `_start_cmd`) and a mutable default argument for `ignore_file_paths`. This design causes all `TemplateBase` instances to share the same state, leading to unexpected interference where changes in one template affect others.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L229-L252 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/b4621eec43adfb1ae10af283b9e9d378a1de2cc8/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L229-L252

Bug: Async Function Blocks Event Loop

 The `__wait_for_build_finish` async function uses `time.sleep()`. This synchronous call blocks the event loop, preventing other async operations from running and degrading performance.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L197-L198 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/f7176a53030816b83222fab7e48aa6cd4e3ef6d3/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L197-L198

Bug: Shared Mutable State Causes Instance Conflicts

 The `TemplateBase` class uses mutable class attributes (`_instructions`, `_base_image`, `_force`, `_force_next_layer`) and a mutable default argument (`ignore_file_paths`). This means all `TemplateBase` instances share the same state, which can cause instructions and other configurations to leak between them.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L235-L252 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/3a30c6e7e2668e4e7c4439a8c63b309d440af532/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L235-L252

Bug: Build Status Polling Fails on 'Waiting'

 The build status polling loop in both the JS and Python SDKs exits early. It only continues for 'building' status, but 'waiting' is also a valid in-progress state. This causes an 'Unknown build error occurred' exception when the build is actually just waiting.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L556-L594 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/466030448f68a2db0e241334aa0319bd2fb15de1/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L556-L594
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L171-L196 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/466030448f68a2db0e241334aa0319bd2fb15de1/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/main.py#L171-L196
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L171-L200 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/466030448f68a2db0e241334aa0319bd2fb15de1/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/main.py#L171-L200

Bug: Glob Pattern Validation Missing

 The `calculate_files_hash` function doesn't validate if the glob pattern finds any files. If no files match, it silently returns a hash containing only the `COPY` instruction, unlike the JavaScript version which throws an error. This can lead to inconsistent template builds or empty tar archives.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L24-L47 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/52e94fae77c3b78dba6ddb1c84a91b24d052076f/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/utils.py#L24-L47

Bug: Timeout Functions Fail on Some Linux Distros

 The `waitForTimeout` and `wait_for_timeout` functions in both JS and Python SDKs pass duration strings (e.g., '20s', '1m') directly to the `sleep` command. This can cause compatibility issues, as some Linux distributions only support numeric seconds for `sleep`, potentially leading to build failures. The SDK should convert these duration strings to plain seconds.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L591-L594 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/88f71e236cb2da7234258a8b3f9f76599de2d599/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L591-L594
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/dockerfile_parser.py#L245-L249 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/88f71e236cb2da7234258a8b3f9f76599de2d599/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/dockerfile_parser.py#L245-L249
packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L249-L252 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/88f71e236cb2da7234258a8b3f9f76599de2d599/packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L249-L252
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L427 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/88f71e236cb2da7234258a8b3f9f76599de2d599/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L427

Bug: Timeout Functions Use Incorrect Time Units

 The `waitForTimeout` (JS) and `wait_for_timeout` (Python) functions expect a timeout in milliseconds but pass the value directly to the `sleep` command, which interprets it as seconds. This results in much longer timeouts than users expect.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L589-L593 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/da771bd6a023ed73281bdd523c4e3567c4ec308c/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L589-L593
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L427 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/da771bd6a023ed73281bdd523c4e3567c4ec308c/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L427

Bug: Timeout Mismatch Causes Excessive Delays

 The `waitForTimeout` and `wait_for_timeout` methods in both SDKs directly use the provided `timeout` value in `sleep` commands. This is an issue because `sleep` expects seconds, but the SDK's typical usage (e.g., `1000`) suggests the input is in milliseconds. This mismatch causes timeouts to be 1000 times longer than intended, significantly impacting test performance.

packages/js-sdk/tests/template/build.test.ts#L20-L21 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/cfaeba02adef0331191f874c6a234605ada4ca81/packages/js-sdk/tests/template/build.test.ts#L20-L21
packages/python-sdk/tests/sync/template_sync/test_build.py#L29-L30 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/cfaeba02adef0331191f874c6a234605ada4ca81/packages/python-sdk/tests/sync/template_sync/test_build.py#L29-L30
packages/python-sdk/tests/async/template_async/test_build.py#L29-L30 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/cfaeba02adef0331191f874c6a234605ada4ca81/packages/python-sdk/tests/async/template_async/test_build.py#L29-L30
packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L590-L593 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/cfaeba02adef0331191f874c6a234605ada4ca81/packages/js-sdk/src/template/index.ts#L590-L593
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L427 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/cfaeba02adef0331191f874c6a234605ada4ca81/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L427

Bug: Floating Point Sleep Inconsistency

 The `wait_for_timeout` method generates `sleep` commands with floating-point durations (e.g., "sleep 10.0"). This can cause the command to fail on systems where `sleep` expects an integer, and creates an inconsistency with the JavaScript SDK.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L428 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/f6200e5d5fe46b70c80f7645cbf36b4576c77363/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/main.py#L424-L428

Bug: Timeout Conversion Inconsistency

 The `wait_for_timeout` (Python) and `waitForTimeout` (JS) functions in the `dockerfileParser` convert timeout milliseconds to seconds using regular division. This can result in fractional `sleep` command arguments, which is inconsistent with other template builder implementations that use integer division or `Math.floor`. This difference leads to unpredictable `sleep` durations (e.g., `sleep 0.5` vs `sleep 0`) and potential issues with shell commands expecting integer values.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L250-L252 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/d5f9ca5d5395dd53e0755d7bccd50090fd12fc1a/packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L250-L252
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/dockerfile_parser.py#L245-L248 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/d5f9ca5d5395dd53e0755d7bccd50090fd12fc1a/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template/dockerfile_parser.py#L245-L248

Bug: Browser Incompatibility with Node.js Static Imports

 Static imports of Node.js modules (`crypto`, `fs`, `path`) in `dockerfileParser.ts` and `utils.ts` cause immediate module resolution failures in browser environments. This prevents the SDK from loading at all, even for functionality intended to work in browsers.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L1-L4 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/9f43bb8b2b710aaf1da8503fd672ab4f70b61fb1/packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L1-L4
packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L2-L3 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/9f43bb8b2b710aaf1da8503fd672ab4f70b61fb1/packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L2-L3

Bug: Build Status Polling Fails on 'Waiting'

 The `waitForBuildFinish` functions incorrectly assume 'building' is the only in-progress status. If the build status API returns 'waiting', the polling loop exits prematurely, leading to an "Unknown build error occurred" instead of continuing to monitor the build.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L163-L164 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/d278e27fb22a506663d1cf6245a1b6fb184d4b4f/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L163-L164
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L163-L164 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/d278e27fb22a506663d1cf6245a1b6fb184d4b4f/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L163-L164
packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L187-L188 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/d278e27fb22a506663d1cf6245a1b6fb184d4b4f/packages/js-sdk/src/template/buildApi.ts#L187-L188

Bug: Build Monitor Fails on "Waiting" Status

 The `wait_for_build_finish` function in both sync and async `build_api.py` exits its loop prematurely. When a build status is "waiting", the `while` loop condition `status == "building"` evaluates to false, causing the function to raise an "Unknown build error occurred" exception instead of continuing to monitor the build.

packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L165-L195 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/a14e7eb8f8058738b2479726ae0c232a3bfbd16c/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_sync/build_api.py#L165-L195
packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L165-L197 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/a14e7eb8f8058738b2479726ae0c232a3bfbd16c/packages/python-sdk/e2b/template_async/build_api.py#L165-L197

Bug: Node.js Modules Fail in Browser

 Top-level Node.js-specific module imports (like `crypto`, `fs`, `path`) in files such as `utils.ts` and `dockerfileParser.ts` cause immediate module loading failures in browser environments. This prevents the SDK from working in browsers and bypasses any intended runtime compatibility checks.

packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L1-L4 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/ea3a650e14820127c80ef1848f2fbf381e602e8b/packages/js-sdk/src/template/utils.ts#L1-L4
packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L6-L8 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/ea3a650e14820127c80ef1848f2fbf381e602e8b/packages/js-sdk/src/template/dockerfileParser.ts#L6-L8

title: Deprecate spawn instead of remove
body:
Adds a deprecated `spawn` command to the CLI, ensuring backward compatibility and guiding users toward the preferred `create` command.


```
$ e2b sandbox
Usage: index sandbox|sbx [options] [command]

work with sandboxes

Options:
  -h, --help                        display help for command

Commands:
  connect|cn <sandboxID>            connect terminal to already running sandbox
  list|ls [options]                 list all sandboxes, by default it list only running ones
  kill|kl [options] [sandboxID]     kill sandbox
  create|cr [options] [template]    create sandbox and connect terminal to it
  logs|lg [options] <sandboxID>     show logs for sandbox
  metrics|mt [options] <sandboxID>  show metrics for sandbox
  help [command]                    display help for command
  ```
  
  ```
$ sbx sp base
Warning: The 'spawn' command is deprecated and will be removed in future releases. Please use 'e2b sandbox create' instead.
Terminal connecting to template base with sandbox ID in897x2dqs7aws8h1x0in
```

reviews.body:
Bug: Sandbox Verification Contract Change

 The `verifySandbox` function now returns true only when the sandbox's state is 'running'. This silently changes its contract from verifying existence and API key association to also requiring a 'running' state. This causes it to return false for valid, non-running sandboxes (like 'stopped' or 'paused'), breaking callers that only need an existence check. The in-code comment no longer reflects this behavior.

apps/web/src/lib/utils.ts#L11-L17 https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/blob/b3d491742adac4af76b10f220964c13a93a31028/apps/web/src/lib/utils.ts#L11-L17

Bug: Sync API Method Fails to Handle Errors

 The `_cls_get_info` method in the sync API client is missing error handling for API `Error` responses, unlike its async counterpart. title: Secured sandbox auto signature
body: Some users report that JS/Python types lack a `useSignature` attribute, which only works when `secure=true` is set for the sandbox. This change removes the `useSignatrue` parameter and makes all upload/download URL requests signed if `secure` was provided, making sure the function will work for all possible cases.
author: sitole

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR simplifies the sandbox file URL signing mechanism by automatically determining when to use signed URLs based on whether the sandbox was created with the `secure` option. It removes the manual `useSignature` parameter from both JavaScript and Python SDKs, making signed URLs the default behavior for secure sandboxes.

- Removes `useSignature` parameter from `downloadUrl` and `uploadUrl` methods
- Automatically enables URL signing when sandbox has an access token (secure mode)
- Updates error messages to clarify that signature expiration requires a secured sandbox

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| packages/js-sdk/src/sandbox/index.ts | Removes `useSignature` parameter and implements automatic signing detection |
| packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox/main.py | Removes `useSignature` parameter and implements automatic signing detection |
| packages/js-sdk/tests/sandbox/secure.test.ts | Updates test to remove `useSignature` parameter usage |
| packages/js-sdk/tests/sandbox/files/signing.test.ts | Updates multiple tests to remove `useSignature` parameter and removes obsolete tests |
| packages/python-sdk/tests/sync/sandbox_sync/files/test_secured.py | Updates test calls to remove `useSignature` parameter |
| packages/python-sdk/tests/async/sandbox_async/files/test_secured.py | Updates test calls to remove `useSignature` parameter |
| apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/filesystem/upload/page.mdx | Updates documentation to remove `useSignature` usage |
| apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/filesystem/download/page.mdx | Updates documentation to remove `useSignature` usage |
| .changeset/wicked-ghosts-tie.md | Adds changelog entry for the breaking change |
</details>
small usability enhancement, otherwise LGTM.

Removed `use_signature` from Python file download/upload calls
Adjust docs with additional info why `secure=true` is here

title: Fixes Sandbox.connect not working when trying to pass API key via parameters
body:
fixes https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/issues/746

- Renamed SandboxApi.get_info() > SandboxApi._cls_get_info()
- Properly overloaded Sandbox.get_info()
- Passed connect parameters to SandboxApi._cls_get_info()
author: mishushakov

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR fixes the issue where `Sandbox.connect` ignored the passed API key by renaming the internal class method, updating the `connect` calls to forward all parameters, and adding overloads for both instance and static `get_info` methods.

- Renamed `SandboxApi.get_info` to `_cls_get_info` in both sync and async API modules.
- Updated `connect` implementations to call `_cls_get_info` with `api_key`, `domain`, `debug`, `request_timeout`, and `proxy` (sync) or the equivalent (async).
- Added typing overloads for `Sandbox.get_info` and `AsyncSandbox.get_info` to support both bound and unbound calls.
- Updated Python and JS tests to use the new static `get_info` signature.

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File                                              | Description                                                 |
| ------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| packages/python-sdk/tests/sync/api_sync/test_sbx_info.py   | Updated test to call static `Sandbox.get_info`               |
| packages/python-sdk/tests/async/api_async/test_sbx_info.py | Updated async test to call static `AsyncSandbox.get_info`    |
| packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/sandbox_api.py        | Renamed `get_info` class method to `_cls_get_info`           |
| packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_sync/main.py               | Updated sync `connect` and `get_info` overloads to use `_cls_get_info` |
| packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/sandbox_api.py       | Renamed async `get_info` class method to `_cls_get_info`     |
| packages/python-sdk/e2b/sandbox_async/main.py              | Updated async `connect` and `get_info` overloads to use `_cls_get_info` |
| packages/js-sdk/tests/api/info.test.ts                      | Changed JS test to call static `Sandbox.getInfo`

title: Fixes parsing http errors in connect client
body:
What was happening is the sandbox was timing out, connect client was not parsing the error correctly (while streaming command) and you got a parsing error instead of sandbox timeout exception

- Fixes https://github.com/e2b-dev/desktop/issues/97 > read response content
- Adds handling for http response status code (such as 502) from the server

Example code:

```py
from e2b import Sandbox
import time

def main():
    sandbox = Sandbox(
        timeout=10,
    )
    info = sandbox.get_info()
    start_time = info.started_at.timestamp()
    while True:
        elapsed_seconds = time.time() - start_time
        sandbox.commands.run("ls -la")
        print(f"Run command at {time.time()}, {elapsed_seconds:.2f} seconds elapsed")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

title: Bump urllib3 from 2.2.3 to 2.5.0 in /packages/python-sdk in the pip group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the /packages/python-sdk directory: [urllib3](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3). Updates `urllib3` from 2.2.3 to 2.5.0
Release notes

Sourced from urllib3's releases.

2.5.0

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Security issues

urllib3 2.5.0 fixes two moderate security issues:

  • Pool managers now properly control redirects when retries is passed — CVE-2025-50181 reported by @​sandumjacob (5.3 Medium, GHSA-pq67-6m6q-mj2v)
  • Redirects are now controlled by urllib3 in the Node.js runtime — CVE-2025-50182 (5.3 Medium, GHSA-48p4-8xcf-vxj5)

Features

  • Added support for the compression.zstd module that is new in Python 3.14. See PEP 784 for more information. (#3610)
  • Added support for version 0.5 of hatch-vcs (#3612)

Bugfixes

  • Raised exception for HTTPResponse.shutdown on a connection already released to the pool. (#3581)
  • Fixed incorrect CONNECT statement when using an IPv6 proxy with connection_from_host. Previously would not be wrapped in []. (#3615)

2.4.0

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Features

  • Applied PEP 639 by specifying the license fields in pyproject.toml. (#3522)
  • Updated exceptions to save and restore more properties during the pickle/serialization process. (#3567)
  • Added verify_flags option to create_urllib3_context with a default of VERIFY_X509_PARTIAL_CHAIN and VERIFY_X509_STRICT for Python 3.13+. (#3571)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed a bug with partial reads of streaming data in Emscripten. (#3555)

Misc

  • Switched to uv for installing development dependecies. (#3550)
  • Removed the multiple.intoto.jsonl asset from GitHub releases. Attestation of release files since v2.3.0 can be found on PyPI. (#3566)

2.3.0

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from urllib3's changelog.

2.5.0 (2025-06-18)

Features

  • Added support for the compression.zstd module that is new in Python 3.14. See PEP 784 <https://peps.python.org/pep-0784/>_ for more information. ([#3610](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3610) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3610>__)
  • Added support for version 0.5 of hatch-vcs ([#3612](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3612) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3612>__)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed a security issue where restricting the maximum number of followed redirects at the urllib3.PoolManager level via the retries parameter did not work.
  • Made the Node.js runtime respect redirect parameters such as retries and redirects.
  • Raised exception for HTTPResponse.shutdown on a connection already released to the pool. ([#3581](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3581) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3581>__)
  • Fixed incorrect CONNECT statement when using an IPv6 proxy with connection_from_host. Previously would not be wrapped in []. ([#3615](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3615) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3615>__)

2.4.0 (2025-04-10)

Features

  • Applied PEP 639 by specifying the license fields in pyproject.toml. ([#3522](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3522) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3522>__)
  • Updated exceptions to save and restore more properties during the pickle/serialization process. ([#3567](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3567) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3567>__)
  • Added verify_flags option to create_urllib3_context with a default of VERIFY_X509_PARTIAL_CHAIN and VERIFY_X509_STRICT for Python 3.13+. ([#3571](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3571) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3571>__)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed a bug with partial reads of streaming data in Emscripten. ([#3555](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3555) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3555>__)

Misc

  • Switched to uv for installing development dependecies. ([#3550](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3550) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3550>__)
  • Removed the multiple.intoto.jsonl asset from GitHub releases. Attestation of release files since v2.3.0 can be found on PyPI. ([#3566](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3566) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3566>__)

2.3.0 (2024-12-22)

... (truncated)

title: Fix(sdk): Export enums as non-const to support `isolatedModules`
body:
Removes the `const` keyword from `FileType` and `FilesystemEventType` enums.

This change prevents the enums from being inlined at compile time, making them compatible with projects that have the `isolatedModules: true` flag enabled in their `tsconfig.json`. This resolves the `Cannot access ambient const enums` build error that occurs in environments like Babel/Vite.
author: ben-fornefeld

title: Add option for a template ready check
body:
## Description
Add option for a template ready check. You can specify `ready_cmd` which will be run until it exits with code 0. Max timeout is 5 minutes. After exit code 0, the template will be marked as ready.

If no ready command is defined, `sleep 20` is assumed when the start command is present, and `sleep 0` when none is defined. It is also possible to define ready command without any start command. This might be helpful if the filesystem contains self-booted services (e.g. using systemd).

```
ready_cmd = "echo 'Starting ready check'; sleep 40; echo 'Template is ready'"
```

This adds possibility to configure the default 20 seconds wait time for a start command.

New documentation can be found at `/docs/sandbox-template/ready-cmd`.

## Checklist
- [x] Make sure the https://github.com/e2b-dev/infra/pull/719 is deployed
author: dobrac

title: Fix: TypeScript union type resolution for Commands.run() method
body:
## Fix TypeScript union type resolution for Commands.run() method

### What's Changed
Improved TypeScript type safety for the `Commands.run()` method by adding proper function overloads to handle the union case when the `background` parameter type is not known at compile time.

### Key Improvements

**Better Type Safety**
- Added a third function overload to correctly handle the `background?: boolean` union case
- TypeScript now correctly infers return types:
  - `background: true` → returns `CommandHandle`
  - `background: false | undefined` → returns `CommandResult`
  - `background: boolean` (unknown at compile time) → returns `CommandHandle | CommandResult`

**Enhanced Developer Experience**
- Added comprehensive JSDoc overloads that clearly document each usage pattern
- IntelliSense now provides accurate type hints and autocompletion
- Eliminates ambiguous union types when the `background` value is known at compile time

**Prevents Runtime Errors**
- When `background` is a literal value, developers get precise types and can't accidentally call wrong methods
- When `background` is a runtime boolean, TypeScript correctly shows the union type requiring proper type narrowing

### Why This Matters for SDK Users

Before this change, developers had to ignore ts warning & manually cast union types when using `commands.run()`:
```typescript
// Before: typescript was complaining, because it expected `true` or `false` as literals. 
// Required manual casting:

// @ts-ignore
const result = await sbx.commands.run('ls', { background: isBackground }) as CommandHandle | CommandResult
```

After this change, TypeScript automatically infers the correct type:
```typescript
// After: TypeScript knows this is CommandHandle
const handle = await sbx.commands.run('ls', { background: true })
await handle.wait() // ✅ TypeScript knows .wait() is available

// TypeScript knows this is CommandResult  
const result = await sbx.commands.run('ls')
console.log(result.stdout) // ✅ TypeScript knows .stdout is available

// When background value is unknown at compile time, returns union type
const isBackground: boolean = Math.random() > 0.5
const result = await commands.run('ls', { background: isBackground })
// result is CommandHandle | CommandResult - requires type narrowing or casting
```
author: ben-fornefeld

reviews.body: Approved, the alternative would be to not use overloads but type inference, however we would be losing docstrings that way

commits.message: refactor: handle run background command boolean handling with union return
chore: remove unecessary function implementation typing
chore: change run command return statement

title: Adds ability to pause Sandbox using Sandbox.pause() static class method
body:
- Added Sandbox.pause static method in JavaScript, Python BETA SDKs
- Added tests for the above

**Example**

JS

```ts
const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
await Sandbox.pause(sbx.sandboxId) // can be used in a background job for example
await Sandbox.resume(sbx.sandboxId)
```

Python

```py
sbx = Sandbox()
Sandbox.pause(sbx.sandbox_id)
Sandbox.resume(sbx.sandbox_id)
```

Add metrics methods to SDKs (#543)

### Description

Consolidate the branches from https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/pull/523,
https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/pull/524 &
https://github.com/e2b-dev/E2B/pull/525

This relates to
[E2B-1311](https://linear.app/e2b/issue/E2B-1311/add-metrics-method-to-sandbox-to-sdks-that-returns-captured-metrics)

### Test
`[js-sdk] pnpm test metrics`
`[python-sdk]` poetry run pytest -n 4 --verbose -x -k "test_metrics"`

### Usage 

#### js-sdk
```js
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
console.log('Sandbox created', sbx.sandboxId)

const metrics = await sbx.getMetrics() 
console.log('Sandbox metrics:', metrics)

/*
[
  {
    cpuCount: 2,
    cpuPct: 50.05,
    memTotalMiB: 484,
    memUsedMiB: 37,
    timestamp: '2025-01-23T23:44:12.222Z'
  },
  {
    cpuCount: 2,
    cpuPct: 4.5,
    memTotalMiB: 484,
    memUsedMiB: 37,
    timestamp: '2025-01-23T23:44:13.220Z'
  }
]
*/
```

#### python-sdk
```py
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox()
print('Sandbox created', sbx.sandbox_id)

metrics = sbx.get_metrics() 
print('Sandbox metrics', metrics) 


# [
#     SandboxMetrics(timestamp=datetime.datetime(2025, 1, 23, 23, 58, 42, 84050, tzinfo=tzutc()), cpu_pct=50.07, cpu_count=2, mem_used_mib=37, mem_total_mib=484), 
#     SandboxMetrics(timestamp=datetime.datetime(2025, 1, 23, 23, 58, 44, 84845, tzinfo=tzutc()), cpu_pct=4.75, cpu_count=2, mem_used_mib=38, mem_total_mib=484),
# ]
```

#### cli
```sh
jonas@mac cli % pnpm build
jonas@mac cli % E2B_DOMAIN=goulash.dev ./dist/index.js sbx sp
jonas@mac cli % E2B_DOMAIN=goulash.dev ./dist/index.js sbx metrics {sandbox_id}

Metrics for sandbox {sandbox_id}

[2025-01-23 00:58:58.829Z]  { cpuCount: 2, cpuPct: 50.21, logger: '', memTotalMiB: 484, memUsedMiB: 38, timestamp: '2025-01-23T00:58:58.829638869Z' }
[2025-01-23 00:59:03.814Z]  { cpuCount: 2, cpuPct: 5.16, logger: '', memTotalMiB: 484, memUsedMiB: 37, timestamp: '2025-01-23T00:59:03.814028031Z' }
[2025-01-23 00:59:08.815Z]  { cpuCount: 2, cpuPct: 1.6, logger: '', memTotalMiB: 484, memUsedMiB: 37, timestamp: '2025-01-23T00:59:08.815933749Z' }
```

title: Add option for proxying SDK requests to API and sandbox
body:
PR that allows you to pass `proxy` (of the type ProxyTypes) when calling methods in the SDK that send requests. Also, if you pass `proxy` when creating a sandbox, all the sandbox requests (called on the Sandbox/AsyncSandbox instance) will automatically use the same proxy.

The PR also contains a small bugfix—adding missing passing of limits and passing these directly instead of through transport to be able to pass the proxy parameter properly.

This change should also be compatible with the code interpreter—it uses the client transport, which uses the passed proxy if there is any.
author: ValentaTomas

Add recursive directory watch (#488)

Adds recursive directory watch to the SDK and documentation section.
Requires merging and deploying https://github.com/e2b-dev/infra/pull/210
first.

### Proposed API change:
**JS**
```js
await sandbox.files.watchDir(dirname, handler, {
  recursive: true
})
```

**Python**
```py
sandbox.files.watch_dir(dirname, recursive=True)
```


---

### Notes

Python SDK was generated using buf.build/protocolbuffers/python:v28.3.
The runtime check was removed for now. Further tests might be required
to make sure the runtime is at least the generated version before
including the runtime check.
Changing the following in the file spec/envd/buf-python.gen.yaml
(generator) allows using different protoc-gen-python version:
From: `plugin: python`
To: `plugin: buf.build/protocolbuffers/python:v28.3`

Fix Socket closed build error (#567)

# Description

If the build and push takes more than 10 minutes the connection gets
automatically closed by load balancer and the request fails with
UND_ERR_SOCKET error. In this case we just need to retry the request.

Add assetPrefix to next config (#569)

This pr adds `assetPrefix` property to the `next.config ` for production
builds. This makes sure that absolute urls instead of relative ones are
used on asset urls in the html markup, which makes it possible to proxy
this app from our upcoming new next app.

Bump the npm_and_yarn group across 1 directory with 2 updates (#562) Bumps the npm_and_yarn group with 2 updates in the /apps/web directory: [@sentry/nextjs](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-javascript) and [next](https://github.com/vercel/next.js). Updates `@sentry/nextjs` from 8.18.0 to 8.52.0
Release notes

Sourced from @​sentry/nextjs's releases.

8.52.0

Important Changes

  • feat(solidstart): Add withSentry wrapper for SolidStart config (#15135)

To enable the SolidStart SDK, wrap your SolidStart Config with withSentry. The sentrySolidStartVite plugin is now automatically added by withSentry and you can pass the Sentry build-time options like this:

import { defineConfig } from
'@solidjs/start/config';
import { withSentry } from '@sentry/solidstart';

export default defineConfig(

withSentry(

{

/* Your SolidStart config options... */

},

{

// Options for setting up source maps

org: process.env.SENTRY_ORG,

project: process.env.SENTRY_PROJECT,

authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN,

},

),

);

With the withSentry wrapper, the Sentry server config should not be added to the public directory anymore. Add the Sentry server config in src/instrument.server.ts. Then, the server config will be placed inside the server build output as instrument.server.mjs.

Now, there are two options to set up the SDK:

  1. (recommended) Provide an --import CLI flag to the start command like this (path depends on your server setup): node --import ./.output/server/instrument.server.mjs .output/server/index.mjs
  2. Add autoInjectServerSentry: 'top-level-import' and the Sentry config will be imported at the top of the server entry (comes with tracing limitations) 
    withSentry(
  {
    /* Your SolidStart config options... */
  },
  {
    // Optional: Install Sentry with a top-level import
    autoInjectServerSentry: 'top-level-import',
  },
);

Other Changes

  • feat(v8/core): Add client outcomes for breadcrumbs buffer (#15149)

... (truncated)

title: Improve assetPrefix configuration for proxy / development setup
body:
Because our docs nextjs app is being proxied by our dashboard nextjs app, we need to ensure the docs nextjs app uses absolute urls to handle it's assets. Because when it is proxied by another nextjs app, the proxy tries to reference the docs nextjs apps assets relatively, which results in assets not being found. 

Before this pr, we set an assetPrefix for every node production deployment, which caused issues on preview deployments, since these can consist of multiple assigned domains and depending on which one was opened, the asset prefixing failed. 

These changes ensure that only the production deployment of the docs application has an assetPrefix. Here we can be certain on which domain it will live. I created a new environment variable `PRODUCTION_URL` here to ensure 100% consistency with `e2b-dev/dashboard`s proxy domain config, without relying on the vercel projects assigned domain setup.
author: ben-fornefeld

title: Make codegen robust and deterministic for js and python SDKs
body:
# Description 

This PR allows to codegen without worrying about your environment and dependencies for consistent outcomes in what code is generated from openapi and envd protobuf spec. Will generate files for `js-sdk` and `python-sdk` 

- [x] create Docker image with all the pinned deps needed to codegen
- [x] create codegen script using this container with mapped volumes
- [x] adapt Makefiles to this new approach
- [x] adapt where we install the connect-python protobuf binary for this to work

# Test

```sh
make codegen # this command is similar to `make generate` but w/o the hassle

Generating SDK code from openapi and envd spec
cd packages/js-sdk && pnpm generate

> e2b@1.2.1 generate /workspace/packages/js-sdk
> python ./../../spec/remove_extra_tags.py sandboxes templates && openapi-typescript ../../spec/openapi_generated.yml -x api_key --arr
ay-length --alphabetize --output src/api/schema.gen.ts

✨ openapi-typescript 7.6.1
🚀 ../../spec/openapi_generated.yml → src/api/schema.gen.ts [44.2ms]
cd packages/js-sdk && pnpm generate-envd-api

> e2b@1.2.1 generate-envd-api /workspace/packages/js-sdk
> openapi-typescript ../../spec/envd/envd.yaml -x api_key --array-length --alphabetize --output src/envd/schema.gen.ts

✨ openapi-typescript 7.6.1
🚀 ../../spec/envd/envd.yaml → src/envd/schema.gen.ts [22.7ms]

[...]

cd packages/python-sdk && make generate-api

All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨
```
author: 0div

commits.message:
* create Docker image with all the pinned deps needed to codegen
* create codegen script using this container with mapped volumes
* adapt Makefiles to this new approach
* adapt where we install the connect-python protobuf binary for this to
  work
use at least 5.29.4 protobuf for python sdk

Fix versions to match updated envd version

Add recursive directory watch (#488)

Adds recursive directory watch to the SDK and documentation section.
Requires merging and deploying https://github.com/e2b-dev/infra/pull/210
first.

### Proposed API change:
**JS**
```js
await sandbox.files.watchDir(dirname, handler, {
  recursive: true
})
```

**Python**
```py
sandbox.files.watch_dir(dirname, recursive=True)
```


---

### Notes

Python SDK was generated using buf.build/protocolbuffers/python:v28.3.
The runtime check was removed for now. Further tests might be required
to make sure the runtime is at least the generated version before
including the runtime check.
Changing the following in the file spec/envd/buf-python.gen.yaml
(generator) allows using different protoc-gen-python version:
From: `plugin: python`
To: `plugin: buf.build/protocolbuffers/python:v28.3`
